Duncan would like it to be modern, but also contain elements of a rustic, cottage garden. Both he and Karen like the idea of a curved patio and wish to avoid decking. Will the couple choose the Rich brothers proposed design, complete with bespoke veg beds, pair of curved hedges and paved courtyard, or will Arit's design that offers a new circular lawn, choice of paved seating areas and a vertical waterfall be more to their taste?(n)

programme.title: New Tricks

However, the cold case copper becomes convinced that the secret to uncovering her real killer lies within the last case she was working on. Picking up where she left off, he travels to the village of Minchampton, where a woman was said to have been murdered by a local lad 25 years previously. Meanwhile, Sasha is left to cope with the investigating officer on the modern-day Miss Marple's murder - an overbearing contemporary from her Hendon training, by the name of DCI Grace Mackie - and Steve lets Gerry stay with him when he is evicted, but soon comes to regret the act of kindness(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: After a successful day of salvaging at the recycling centre in Altrincham, Greater Manchester, upcycling expert Sarah Moore tasks carpenter Norman Wilkinson with reimagining an old wall cabinet. While furniture designer Rupert Blanchard is hoping he can create a top end piece from a pair of metal bench ends, Sarah takes on the challenge of restyling a fashion mannequin(n)

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys invites comedian Elis James, Love Island's Montana Brown, actress Madeline Smith and Casualty star George Rainsford to take to the black chair to be put to the test on their chosen specialist subjects, which are the Welsh football team, Desperate Housewives, Kew Gardens and the Back to the Future trilogy. The second round sees them tackling general knowledge questions in a bid to carry off the commemorative trophy(n)

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman invite another selection of famous faces to take part in the unorthodox general knowledge quiz. As always, the challengers are tasked with coming up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public. This time, all the contestants come from the world of journalism, as Owen Jones, Paris Lees, Kay Burley, Adam Boulton, Carole Malone, Quentin Letts, Amol Rajan and Victoria Derbyshire take part(n)

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman present the first live episode of this year's series, with a line-up featuring the likes of Alex Scott, Emma Barton and James Cracknell. Having had only two weeks to rehearse their first routine, the 15 couples take to the ballroom floor for the first time. The celebrities are dancing to impress the viewers and the four judges, new judge Motsi Mabuse, Bruno Tonioli, Craig Revel Horwood and Head Judge Shirley Ballas(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Hospital drama with the staff of Holby's accident and emergency department. Taking his own advice with reassurance from Ruby and Jan, Iain decides to join the HEMS team. Meanwhile, Rash's career is in jeopardy when he gets blamed for a mistake made by Mason, and Ethan's fascination with Effie deepens(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Manchester City v Watford at the Etihad Stadium and Burnley v Norwich City at Turf Moor. This was the first meeting between the Citizens and Hornets since the FA Cup final in May, when Pep Guardiola's men ran riot in a thumping 6-0 victory at Wembley Stadium. That was City's 11th successive victory over Watford, a record stretching back to April 2007. Plus, action from Everton v Sheffield United at Goodison Park, Leicester City v Tottenham Hotspur at the King Power Stadium, Newcastle United v Brighton & Hove Albion at St James' Park, and Southampton v Bournemouth, which took place on Friday night at St Mary's Stadium(n)

programme.title: The Rap Game UK
programme.desc: In the first challenge, online influencers offer the remaining artists their honest feedback, with online presence, lyrics, personalities and sense of style all under the microscope. For the second challenge, the contestants are paired-up with an esteemed video director to shoot a music video(n)

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by former players Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to present highlights of Thursday night's game - Jacksonville Jaguars v Tennessee Titans at TIAA Bank Field(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Chris Mason presents a round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Manchester City v Watford at the Etihad Stadium and Burnley v Norwich City at Turf Moor. This was the first meeting between the Citizens and Hornets since the FA Cup final in May, when Pep Guardiola's men ran riot in a thumping 6-0 victory at Wembley Stadium. That was City's 11th successive victory over Watford, a record stretching back to April 2007. Plus, action from Everton v Sheffield United at Goodison Park, Leicester City v Tottenham Hotspur at the King Power Stadium, Newcastle United v Brighton & Hove Albion at St James' Park, and Southampton v Bournemouth, which took place on Friday night at St Mary's Stadium(n)

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00(n)

programme.title: Sunday Politics North West
programme.desc: The latest political news and debate from the region(n)

programme.title: Sunday Morning Live
programme.desc: Topical debate covering religious, moral and ethical issues, with guest panellists and contributions from viewers. Presented by Sean Fletcher and Ria Hebden(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under Revisited
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman catches up with the Mungur family, who decided to spend a trial week living in Brisbane, Australia 12 months earlier, to see whether they made the move on a permanent basis(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Philip Serrell and Nick Hall lend their antiques expertise to two teams in Lewes, East Sussex, as they compete to make a profit at auction. Presented by Anita Manning(n)

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: The Rev Kate Bottley visits Norwich Cathedral to see how a 50ft helter-skelter is attracting families, allowing them to see the ceiling sculptures and bringing the Bible to life(n)

programme.title: Points of View
programme.desc: Viewers' opinions on BBC shows and behind-the-scenes reports on new programmes(n)

programme.title: Lifeline
programme.desc: Gaby Roslin presents a lifeline appeal on behalf of WellChild - a charity helping to get seriously ill children and young people out of hospital and home to their families(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell explore a one-bedroom flat high on potential in London's Streatham, a coastal bungalow in Cumbria and a semi-detached property near Derby with scope for an extension. The hosts see what each went for at auction and talk to the new owners' about their plans for their aquisitions(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin is in Norfolk to help a couple with an £800,000 budget buy their first property together, and rides on a narrow gauge steam train when he meets the enthusiasts keeping the Bure Valley Railway running(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers are tasked with transforming a long, thin garden in Northampton, and decide to create a multi-functional space the whole family can enjoy(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jay Blades saves a battered suitcase, a wingback chair and a Formica table top from the recycling centre, and tries to transform them with the help of Daniel Heath and Ray Clarke(n)

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present a musical theatre edition of the general knowledge quiz, in which four teams try to come up with the answers that nobody else could think of. Featuring Ray Quinn, Joanne Clifton, Ben Forster, Kerry Ellis, Jenna Russell, Hannah Waddingham, Duncan James and Jade Ewen(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: The programme plays host to One Man and His Dog 2019, as the best shepherds from across the British Isles descend on the Scottish Borders to find out which nation has what it takes to claim this coveted title. Matt Baker and Helen Skelton present proceedings from Bowhill House, as the best youngsters and senior shepherds from England, Wales, Ireland and Scotland are teamed up in a bid to win the trophy for their country(n)

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce heads to the waterfront beside the V&A Dundee design museum, on the east coast of Scotland. She explores the fascinating story behind the landmark museum and the treasures it houses. Among the discoveries are a valuable bowl found at a car-boot sale for just £3, a set of rare SAS medals and a Vivienne Westwood jacket(n)

programme.title: Peaky Blinders
programme.desc: News of Tommy's activities has reached Winston Churchill. At a family meeting, Tommy lays out his plans for Mosley, which will take place at a rally Sir Oswald is leading. Has he underestimated his opponent? Tommy is surprised by another strategy coming from an unexpected corner, and he also has his own secrets to reveal. Drama, starring Cillian Murphy, Sam Claflin, Paul Anderson, Helen McCrory and Cosmo Jarvis(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman presents action from today's Premier League fixtures, including Chelsea v Liverpool at Stamford Bridge, as the Blues looked to avenge last season's 2-1 defeat here. Plus, Crystal Palace v Wolverhampton Wanderers at Selhurst Park, West Ham United v Manchester United at London Stadium, Arsenal v Aston Villa at Emirates Stadium, and a round-up of yesterday's goals(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Burnley defender James Tarkowski, Great Britain sprinter Asha Philip, IPC World Championships long jump champion Stef Reid and Olympic silver medal-winning swimmer Michael Jamieson join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Izzie Balmer and James Braxton's penultimate leg takes them to Lowestoft and Cromer. Along the way, Izzie is charmed by a snake and an art deco necklace, while James goes crabbing and gets all fired up by a cannonball. But who will come out on top at auction in Banbury?(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: The Rich Brothers and Charlie Dimmock compete to create a garden in Chester that combines exotic holiday memories of Borneo, America and South Africa for owners Michelle and Andrew(n)

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: With the bank holiday weekend looming, Monty Don has plenty of inspiration for gardeners from planting for late summer colour to tidying up herbs and pruning soft fruit. Nick Bailey concludes his journey into the clematis world by looking at the late summer-flowering varieties and the programme travels to Devon to meet a retired biology teacher with a passion for houseleeks(n)

programme.title: Nadiya's Time to Eat
programme.desc: Nadiya Hussain demonstrates recipes to take the stress out of impressing guests, including raspberry cheesecake croissants, a speedy satay chicken tray bake and a fuss-free cheat's trifle with creamy chia seeds, yoghurt, fresh fruit and sweet brioche. In Walsall, Nadiya's shortcut curry with spicy jackfruit and speedy naan gives a novice cook the family dinner party that she's always dreamed of(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond(n)

programme.title: Hardball
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Ore Oduba in which contestants have to answer questions to get as far along a track as they can before a ball catches and passes them, ending their go(n)

programme.title: Home Away from Home
programme.desc: Friends from Cheshire swap homes and lifestyles with a Derbyshire couple, with all of them reporting afterward on how they found the experience. Presented by Gyles Brandreth(n)

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: The team tries to find relatives of a travelling salesman who lived in Scotland. Meanwhile, the search for heirs of a retired dressmaker uncovers tales of boxing and bigamy, and the tragic story of a family decimated by the holocaust. Plus, details of two unclaimed estates where heirs still need to be found(n)

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Alice Roberts visits archaeological digs around the UK, starting with the west of Britain. Finds include the camp from which Vikings invaded Britain and groundbreaking new evidence that the world famous Avebury stone circle isn't just a sacred site, but a place where our ancestors lived and worked(n)

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Actress Sheridan Smith, who went from singing with her parents in the working men's clubs near their North Lincolnshire home to successful stage performances in Little Shop of Horrors and Legally Blonde - not to mention her countless TV appearances - embarks on a journey to find out where her family's musical talents come from. She is soon hot on the trail of her great-great-grandfather Benjamin Doubleday, a world-class banjo player and musical impresario - but she is surprised by his change in fortunes(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: At Norwich Cathedral's great nave, experts Kate Bateman and David Fletcher pick out some choice items to take to auction. Among them are an enamel-topped silver trinket box and a vintage model car. Plus, Paul Martin uncovers the meaning of religious graffiti(n)

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize(n)

programme.title: Sam & Shauna's Big Cook Out
programme.desc: Samantha Evans and Shauna Guinn travel to the small town of Deri near Caerphilly to celebrate the success of its rugby club, which has gone from the brink of extinction just four years ago to being crowned league champions. To mark the achievements of the players and the volunteers who run the club, Sam and Shauna cook up a huge venison feast with their help(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Metal worker Dominic Chinea has to use all of his restoration skills to future proof an old, Cornish museum sign, while Steven Fletcher has to steer an old banger back onto the road. Plus, bear-care team Julie and Amanda start treatment on George, a very special teddy who has lost one of his eyes and his growl(n)

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys grills four more contenders on their chosen subjects, which are the history of the London Underground, the 1969 Woodstock festival, the industrialist William Armstrong and the life and films of Al Pacino. They are then each afforded two minutes to demonstrate their prowess on general knowledge(n)

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don assesses the progress, successes and failures of the paradise garden he planted at the start of the year. He also sets about sowing a meadow of wildflowers in the orchard and gives advice on what to plant now in the vegetable garden. Frances Tophill travels to Glasgow to meet a gardener whose hands-off approach to his garden has reaped rewards for wildlife, Adam Frost visits a garden in Wales where a potentially unpromising and rocky site has been transformed, while Mark Lane explores Nymans in West Sussex to find out how the garden is managed with sustainability at its heart(n)

programme.title: Inside the Vatican
programme.desc: A film crew gains exceptional access to Vatican, afforded a year to examine its inner workings. Cameras capture everyone including Pope Francis himself, his head of security, the nuns who cater for the homeless, the choristers of the Sistine Chapel, the papal gardener and the chief of the diplomatic corps to gain a unique and exclusive insight into the lives of those who live and work in this independent City State. The first of two programmes follows the build-up to Easter, when tens of thousands of pilgrims arrive and Pope Francis takes centre stage in the world's biggest theatre of faith(n)

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig 'quarrels', with regular panellist Alan Davies and guest players Jason Manford, Aisling Bea and Anuvab Pal at the brunt of her angst. As usual, the questions are so difficult points are instead awarded for the answers the host finds most interesting as opposed to correct(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's national and international news stories, with Katie Razzall(n)

programme.title: Front Row Late
programme.desc: Mary Beard talks to novelist Margaret Atwood as her sequel to The Handmaid's Tale - The Testaments - is published, Their far-reaching conversation touches upon how Gilead has changed almost 35 years on from the original book and how the cloak featured in the TV adaptation of The Handmaid's Tale has become a symbol of protest around the world. Plus, Atwood shares her responses to the current political climate, about the accusation that she is a 'bad feminist'; and the hype surrounding the release of this new book(n)

programme.title: The Lunchbox (2013)
programme.desc: A neglected wife hopes to get some appreciation from her husband by making him packed lunches containing love letters and sending them to his workplace. However, the packages are accidentally given to the wrong employee, who responds to the messages and becomes a close and trusted confidante. Romantic drama, starring Irrfan Khan and Nimrat Kaur. In Hindi and English(n)

programme.title: The Chefs' Brigade
programme.desc: Jason Atherton takes the brigade to San Sebastian in the Basque region of Spain, where they face their toughest challenge to date competing against the kitchen staff of Michelin-starred chef Paulo Airaudo. Jason has had to bring in two new recruits to replace the chefs who have dropped out, and they struggle to find their place on the team - especially as both are used to being in charge(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: CBeebies Prom: Off to the Moon
programme.desc: To celebrate 50 years since the first astronauts walked on the moon, the CBeebies Prommers head to the Royal Albert Hall to take a musical journey to the moon. Featuring music performed by the orchestra Chineke! conducted by Kwamé Ryan, with additional performances by the Chineke! Junior Orchestra and the first ever CBeebies Proms Choir, the pre-school audience can hear pieces by composers such as Handel, Puccini, John Adams, Benjamin Britten and Jessie Montgomery as well as the world premiere of Hans Zimmer's new work, Earth(n)

programme.title: Ali-A's Superchargers
programme.desc: Lancashire lad Brandan loves cars that have been modified, so naturally finds his mother's run-down car really boring. However, Ali-A's Supercharging team can soon fixes that(n)

programme.title: Mustangs FC
programme.desc: The grudge match against the Wildcats has everyone feeling the pressure. Comedy drama about a group of girls who start their own football team(n)

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: Top sport legends are put through their paces with some action-packed challenges. Plus, the winner of the Royal Garden competition is revealed as the programme catches up with the competition's judge, the Duchess of Cambridge(n)

programme.title: Curious Creatures
programme.desc: Kate Humble hosts as team captains Chris Packham and Lucy Cooke are joined by Vianet Djenguet and Steve Backshall for another test of wildlife knowledge(n)

programme.title: Super Senses: The Secret Power of Animals
programme.desc: Biologist Patrick Aryee and physicist Helen Czerski continue their exploration of the world of animal senses by investigating sound - from the deepest rumbles to the highest squeaks - using specialised technology to detect noises beyond the range of human hearing. They reveal how alligators use low-pitch sounds to make the water around them 'dance', show what it is like to be a bat and see with echo-location, and convert a classic camper van into a giant speaker to conduct the ultimate hearing test for a herd of elephants(n)

programme.title: Robot Wars
programme.desc: Robots Expulsion, Coyote, Thor, Magnetar, Hobgoblin and Push to Exit battle it out for a place in the grand final. Among the players controlling the machines are self-taught engineer Ellis, and a team of schoolchildren determined to prove lack of experience is not a problem. Presented by Dara Ó Briain and Angela Scanlon(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin visits a semi-detached house just outside of Canterbury in Kent, and a mid-terrace in Wolverhampton. Martin Roberts visits a project in Crewe, Cheshire(n)

programme.title: Hairy Bikers: Route 66
programme.desc: Dave Myers and Si King visit a biker bar in Missouri to find out about the origins of Route 66, before heading west, where they visit a roadside museum described as a love letter to the road. They also stop off for lunch in Kansas, sample Yorkshire pudding wraps in Tulsa and finally reach rural Oklahoma, where they cook with members of the Cherokee nation(n)

programme.title: My Life on a Plate
programme.desc: Brian Turner invites actor and TV presenter Christopher Biggins to reminisce about his youth in Salisbury. He describes a flamboyant childhood, where he developed an early taste for the high life and was taught the appreciation of food by larger-than-life aunts and grannies. The chef then cooks two nostalgic, mouth-watering dishes, hoping to transport Biggins back to happy times(n)

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: Profiles of the legendary leading ladies of Hollywood, featuring the likes of Lauren Bacall, Natalie Wood, Debbie Reynolds, Joan Crawford and Bette Davis. Sylvia Syms narrates(n)

programme.title: Death Defying Acts (2007)
programme.desc: Renowned escapologist Harry Houdini embarks on a tour of Scotland, where a con woman claims she is a psychic and will contact his dead mother in return for a $10,000 reward. However, things take an unexpected turn when Houdini begins a passionate affair with the fraudster. Period drama, with Guy Pearce, Catherine Zeta-Jones, Timothy Spall and Saoirse Ronan(n)

programme.title: The Best of British Takeaways
programme.desc: Tom Kerridge celebrates the UK's favourite takeaways, beginning with a look at what makes the best fish and chips and the trade secrets behind them. Meanwhile, Cherry Healey discovers that the origins of our national dish aren't actually British at all(n)

programme.title: James May's Cars of the People
programme.desc: The presenter examines how the car industry in Germany and Japan blossomed following the Second World War, becoming manufacturing giants that far outstripped the Allied nations that had defeated them. He test drives some of the vehicles Germany and Japan produced during the post-war period, as well as an assortment of other classic cars, including E-Types and Mustangs. He also reveals the sad tale of the Austin Allegro, a vehicle that had a devastating impact on the British car industry(n)

programme.title: Golf: PGA Championship
programme.desc: Eilidh Barbour presents third-round action from Wentworth, where the likes of Rory McIlroy, Justin Rose, Shane Lowry and US duo Patrick Reed and Tony Finau were all hoping to have made the cut. The event, on Wentworth's famous West Course, has been moved from May to September, which has resulted in a stellar global field being assembled. Last year, 2018 Open champion Francesco Molinari shot a third-round 66 to move into a share of the lead with 2014 winner McIlroy. Gusty winds and fast, firm greens made scoring difficult but Molinari managed a bogey-free round featuring six birdies to play himself firmly into contention. With commentary from Peter Alliss, Ken Brown and Andrew Cotter(n)

programme.title: Dad's Army
programme.desc: A daring bid to rescue Walker and Godfrey from a stricken pumping station ends with Mainwaring and the entire Walmington-on-Sea platoon trapped and facing the prospect of drowning when a burst pipe floods the building. Comedy first aired in 1972, starring Arthur Lowe and John Le Mesurier(n)

programme.title: Pubs, Ponds and Power: The Story of the Village
programme.desc: Archaeologist Ben Robinson explores the history of Lavenham in Suffolk, which was one of the wealthiest places in England in the 16th century due to its woollen cloth industry. The rich abandoned the village after this business declined and by the 19th century it had become a place of poverty(n)

programme.title: Rise of the Nazis
programme.desc: The final episode examines the infamous Night of the Long Knives in late June and early July of 1934, when Adolf Hitler - urged on by Hermann Goering and Heinrich Himmler - ordered a series of political executions on those seen as a threat to the Nazi regime. The most well-known of these was Ernst Roehm, commander and co-founder of the Sturmabteilung, the Nazi Party's militia, whom Hitler had come to see as a potential rival. At least 85 people died during the purge, although the final death toll may have been in the hundreds(n)

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig is joined by panellists Loyiso Gola, Jason Manford, Sarah Millican and Alan Davies to answer questions about quirky topics. As ever, the panellists will not necessarily be expected to deliver the correct answers, and Sandi will be handing out points to those who provide the 'most interesting' guesses(n)

programme.title: Nomad: In the Footsteps of Bruce Chatwin
programme.desc: Documentary in which Werner Herzog sets out on a journey inspired by his friend, writer and adventurer Bruce Chatwin, who had a passion for the nomadic life. Carrying the rucksack that Chatwin gave him on his deathbed, Herzog travels to South America, Australia and the Welsh borders, uncovering stories of lost tribes, wanderers and dreamers(n)

programme.title: The Talented Mr Ripley (1999)
programme.desc: A man agrees to travel to Italy and persuade a businessman's spoiled son to return home. Before long he develops a taste for the high life, and decides to get in on the action - first by befriending the rich and beautiful people, and then by taking the drastic measure of assuming his new-found friend's identity. Anthony Minghella's thriller, starring Matt Damon, Jude Law, Gwyneth Paltrow and Cate Blanchett(n)

programme.title: In Order of Disappearance (2014)
programme.desc: A timid, law-abiding snow plough driver refuses to believe that his son's death was caused by a heroin overdose, and goes in search of his killers. His investigation leads to him accidentally becoming a feared vigilante and sparking off a gang war. Dark comedy thriller, starring Stellan Skarsgard and Kristofer Hivju. In Norwegian, Swedish, Danish, English, Serbian and German(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by former players Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to present highlights of Thursday night's game - Jacksonville Jaguars v Tennessee Titans at TIAA Bank Field(n)

programme.title: Life in a Cottage Garden with Carol Klein
programme.desc: During March and April, the horticulturist clears the detritus left behind by winter, as celandines, violets and primroses begin to bloom. She plants the first seeds of the year and is joined by a local gardener to prune the box hedge, but their work is interrupted by a nesting sparrow(n)

programme.title: A to Z of TV Gardening
programme.desc: Carol Kirkwood looks at subjects beginning with the letters X, Y and Z, concluding her alphabetical journey through the world of horticulture. Along the way, she gets inspiration and advice from some of the BBC's most popular gardening presenters(n)

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don assesses the progress, successes and failures of the paradise garden he planted at the start of the year. He also sets about sowing a meadow of wildflowers in the orchard and gives advice on what to plant now in the vegetable garden. Frances Tophill travels to Glasgow to meet a gardener whose hands-off approach to his garden has reaped rewards for wildlife, Adam Frost visits a garden in Wales where a potentially unpromising and rocky site has been transformed, while Mark Lane explores Nymans in West Sussex to find out how the garden is managed with sustainability at its heart(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison meets a larger-than-life character in Dorset - The Cerne Abbas Giant. It's 100 years since the Giant was gifted to the National Trust, and he needs to be smartened up, so Ellie joins a group of volunteers to re-chalk the figure, hoping to restore it to its former glory. Steve Brown visits the world's only managed flock of Mute swans at Abbotsbury Swannery, while Adam Henson meets the Welsh and Scottish contenders hoping to lift this year's One Man and His Dog trophy(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights of the series, recalling his favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen(n)

programme.title: The Lady Vanishes (1938)
programme.desc: A traveller strikes up a conversation with a scatterbrained elderly lady when their train is delayed, but after the journey resumes, her new friend mysteriously vanishes. Almost everyone else on board pleads ignorance about the matter, prompting her and a fellow passenger to investigate the intriguing occurrence. Alfred Hitchcock's mystery, starring Margaret Lockwood, Michael Redgrave, May Whitty, Paul Lukas and Cecil Parker(n)

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: A look at the television appearances made over the years by pioneering Hollywood film director Alfred Hitchcock, capturing the milestones and highlights of his life and career. Narrated by Sylvia Syms(n)

programme.title: Live Cycling
programme.desc: Clare Balding presents live coverage of the inaugural mixed time-trial relay, which features a 28km circuit starting and finishing in Harrogate, with three male and female riders from each participating country battling it out for the gold. Plus, highlights of Saturday's para-cycling race(n)

programme.title: Animals in Love
programme.desc: In the second of two programmes about relationships in the wild, Liz Bonnin explores the ways in which animals get together. She observes a form of extreme flirting in a troop of capuchin monkeys in South America, which sees the females go to extraordinary lengths to attract the alpha male, reveals how African hyenas find it tough getting a girl and how an orang-utan in America chooses its partner in a very 21st-century way. For other animals, it turns out smell is a deal-breaker, while for others, it's all about having the best moves on the dance floor(n)

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: A lifeboat crew in Dorset faces the challenge of getting urgent medical aid to a rock climber, who has fallen more than 40 feet and landed on an inaccessible rocky ledge. In Cornwall, a paraglider misjudges his launch and ends up crashing into the sea, while the Redcar crew search for a man who has drifted over a mile offshore in a small toy dinghy(n)

programme.title: Golf: PGA Championship
programme.desc: Eilidh Barbour presents action from the fourth and final day at Wentworth, where Francesco Molinari was victorious last year. The Italian started the day with a share of the lead alongside Rory McIlroy, but his final four-under-par round of 68 helped him to finish two strokes clear of the Northern Irishman and claim a first prize of nearly 1million euros. With commentary from Peter Alliss, Ken Brown and Andrew Cotter(n)

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: In the wake of the disruption at Gatwick last December, high-risk specialist Aldo Kane investigates the scale of the threat that drones pose to UK airspace and airports - from rogue hobbyists to terrorist attacks. He explores what technology is out there that governments and the aviation industry can turn to keep the skies safe(n)

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: A Yorkshire shepherd and his business partner hope the Dragons will flock to invest in their field-to-fork lamb delivery boxes. An inclusive fashion designer brings a little street style to the Den and a confectionery pioneer hopes to bag a sweet Dragon deal with his low sugar, dairy-free chocolate(n)

programme.title: Stacey Dooley Investigates
programme.desc: The presenter meets bounty hunters in the US, who are hired to track down fugitives who have skipped bail on behalf of the bondsman. Most of the American states grant these freelance operatives tremendous legal power and freedom to act. However, they receive little training, and almost anyone can go into business as a bounty hunter. The programme also explores the workings of the commercial bail system(n)

programme.title: State of the Union
programme.desc: Comedy written by Nick Hornby and directed by Stephen Frears, about a couple who fear their marriage is failing and decide to give counselling sessions a try. Starring Chris O'Dowd and Rosamund Pike(n)

programme.title: State of the Union
programme.desc: Comedy written by Nick Hornby and directed by Stephen Frears, about a couple who fear their marriage is failing and decide to give counselling sessions a try. Starring Chris O'Dowd and Rosamund Pike(n)

programme.title: The Mash Report
programme.desc: Nish Kumar and a cast of comedy correspondents including Ellie Taylor and Steve N Allen present the satirical and surreal take on the week's news(n)

programme.title: Love in the Countryside
programme.desc: Sara Cox helps more people living and working in remote rural areas to find love. The first episode introduces Grace, a sheep farmer from Wales, and Martin, a dairy farmer from Yorkshire. Grace faces inheriting her father's business when he retires, so is looking for a partner who will be willing to share the responsibility. Martin has not had a relationship in more than a decade, so leaves behind his Doncaster roots to attend a speed dating event in London attended by women from across the UK who have written to the show(n)

programme.title: The Big Hospital Experiment
programme.desc: The young volunteers begin their third week at the Royal Derby Hospital and Senior Nurse Karen gives them more responsibilities, as she sends four volunteers to two new wards. One of the youngest volunteers on the programme is Fin, who, at 19 years old, wasn't sensitive enough to the needs on the Cancer Ward and was asked to leave. Fin and Michael are moved to the Elderly Care Ward, where they are tasked with providing care for 101-year-old patient John, a wartime Spitfire pilot(n)

programme.title: World News Today
programme.desc: The day's leading stories(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1988
programme.desc: Mike Read and Gary Davies introduce the edition first aired on June 16, featuring performances by Sabrina, Erasure, the Sisters of Mercy, the Fat Boys and Chubby Checker, Eurythmics, a-ha, Sade, Matt Bianco, Bros, the Timelords and the Pasadenas(n)

programme.title: A Musical History
programme.desc: Fleetwood Mac are the great survivors of British and American rock music. For more than 50 years they've overcome break-ups and breakdowns to become one of the most successful bands of all time, selling over 100 million records worldwide, with their 1977 smash Rumours accounting for nearly half of those sales. Here, fan Edith Bowman provides a narrative overview, alongside fellow celebrity admirers who all pay tribute to the band. Contributors include KT Tunstall, Travis' frontman Fran Healy, Toyah Willcox, Sian Pattenden and Emma Dabiri(n)

programme.title: Fleetwood Mac's Songbird: Christine McVie
programme.desc: While she has never fronted Fleetwood Mac, preferring to align herself with 'the boys' in the rhythm section, Christine McVie is their most successful singer-songwriter whose hits include Over My Head, Don't Stop and Everywhere. After global success in the late '70s and mid-'80s Christine left the band in 1998, living in semi-retirement in Kent, only to rejoin in 2014. Here, she finally gets to take centre stage to tell both her own story and that of Fleetwood Mac from her vantage point. Contributors include Christine's current bandmates Stevie Nicks, Mick Fleetwood, John McVie, Neil Finn and Mike Campbell(n)

programme.title: Fleetwood Mac - Don't Stop
programme.desc: Documentary looking back over the band's long career, from the early blues outfit led by Peter Green in the late 1960s, to their reincarnation in the 1970s with the line-up that went on to record the 40-million-selling album Rumours. Mick Fleetwood, John McVie, Lindsey Buckingham and Stevie Nicks reveal the truth behind the successes, failures and relationships that characterised their music and made them one of the most enduring acts in popular music(n)

programme.title: Totally British: 70s Rock 'n' Roll
programme.desc: Part two of two. A collection of archive performances by artists including Dr Feelgood, Ducks Deluxe, Elvis Costello, the Motors, Steve Gibbons Band, Dire Straits, Graham Parker and the Rumour, Eddie and the Hot Rods, Ace, Dave Edmunds and Nick Lowe from TV shows such as The Old Grey Whistle Test and Top of the Pops(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1988
programme.desc: Mike Read and Gary Davies introduce the edition first aired on June 16, featuring performances by Sabrina, Erasure, the Sisters of Mercy, the Fat Boys and Chubby Checker, Eurythmics, a-ha, Sade, Matt Bianco, Bros, the Timelords and the Pasadenas(n)

programme.title: Peaky Blinders
programme.desc: Arthur leads the Peaky Blinders in their plot to take over London's Eden Club, and an angry Sabini manages to convince Alfie to put their feud to one side and help him destroy the gang. Tommy hires society darling May Carleton to train his race horse and they quickly form a close relationship, and he later discovers the truth about the mission he is being forced to undertake for Major Campbell. Is he on the verge of losing everything as he becomes embroiled in Campbell's political game? Back in Birmingham, Polly's son Michael soon discovers the dark side of the family business(n)

programme.title: Peaky Blinders
programme.desc: Tommy's power base in London is obliterated and Arthur and Michael are imprisoned as Major Campbell makes his influence felt. Distraught at the prospect of losing her son to a lengthy spell in prison, Polly compromises herself to secure Michael's freedom. However, her actions risk damaging already fragile relationships. While attempting to regain the upper hand, Tommy is paid a visit by someone from his past. Old wounds are reopened and he finds himself having to make an impossible decision. Starring Cillian Murphy and Sam Neill(n)

programme.title: Peaky Blinders
programme.desc: It is the day of the Epsom Derby and Tommy is there to carry out the mission given to him by Major Campbell, but he plans to turn the situation to his advantage by bringing his gang to the racecourse and striking a blow against Sabini. However, more than one of his adversaries have forged secret schemes, and as he readies his troops, Tommy receives shocking news that will change everything. Period gangster drama, starring Cillian Murphy and Sam Neill(n)

programme.title: Britain's Secret Seas
programme.desc: Paul Rose, Tooni Mahto and Frank Pope conduct an exploration of marine life in the North Sea, and how it has been affected by the construction of a wind farm off the Norfolk coast. They also observe England's largest colony of grey seals in the Farne Islands, find out why Bridlington, East Yorkshire, has enjoyed a particularly bountiful lobster harvest and learn more about how fish behave when threatened by predators(n)

programme.title: Life in the Air
programme.desc: The series turns to animals that fly, including footage of half a million mother bats, an agile sparrowhawk raiding for food, and large beetles taking to the sky as part of their mating ritual. The programme also reveals how peregrine falcons are able to reach speeds of 200mph, and why an albatross's nose helps it to fly. Narrated by Suranne Jones(n)

programme.title: Peaky Blinders

programme.title: Peaky Blinders
programme.desc: On the orders of the secretive Economic League, Tommy goes to London to meet the wildly eccentric and volatile Duke Leon - and when he is given his mission, he soon realises the extreme lengths his new paymasters are willing to go to in their quest for power. Back in Birmingham, John's temper gets the better of him, and a personal dispute with the Italians threatens to explode into a devastating war. Tommy struggles to regain control of the situation - only to discover how extensive his new enemy's hold on the Peaky Blinders is. Cillian Murphy, Helen McCrory and Joe Cole star, with an appearance by Paddy Considine(n)

programme.title: Peaky Blinders
programme.desc: Tommy responds to the Italians' actions, setting him on a path of deadly vengeance that threatens to take him to his darkest place yet - and could also splinter the family. And when he meets Grand Duchess Izabella, the real force behind his mission, he discovers there's a traitor in his paymasters' midst - one who could unravel the entire plan. Meanwhile, Polly realises the depths of her new friend's intentions for her, and Linda implores an increasingly guilty Arthur to give up the criminal life(n)

programme.title: The Last Night of the Proms from Around the UK
programme.desc: YolanDa Brown and Josie d'Arby introduce a whistle stop tour around the UK, as the nations join in the Last Night of the Proms celebrations. Josie is at Swansea's Singleton Park with 80s pop sensations ABC, West End star Sophie Evans, 2019 BBC Cardiff Singer of the World Andrei Kymach, and BBC Young Jazz Musician 2018 winner, saxophonist Xhosa Cole. At Glasgow Green, Barbara Dickson entertains the crowds alongside Scottish soprano Eleanor Dennis and violinist Stephanie Childress, while Susan Boyle headlines at Titanic Slipways, Belfast, and alongside her in the line-up are Australian tenor Mark Vincent and award-winning violinist Ziyu He. The gang tries everything to wake her up but nothing works, not even a kiss from brave Sir Bevel. Can Bitz invent a way to save the day before Zipsy and Popsy put a spell on them too?(n)

programme.title: The Furchester Hotel
programme.desc: A fabulous peacock called Mr Flash comes to stay at the hotel. Everyone is in awe of his feathers, but he claims he doesn't want his feathers ruffled(n)

programme.title: Something Special

programme.title: Footy Pups
programme.desc: Rachel Yankey and the Footy Pups jump to it, while at Pupton Park Geraldine worries she cannot jump high enough as she is the smallest on the team(n)

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.desc: The bunny tells a curious Cotton-tail how he and Squirrel Nutkin became friends, and ends up going on a quest to Owl Island to retrieve his pal's tail(n)

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: Grandmaster Glitch makes the Statue of Liberty fall, right into the path of a passing ferry(n)

programme.title: Go Jetters

programme.title: Sarah & Duck

programme.title: Swashbuckle
programme.desc: Gem and her teams of Swashbucklers try to win her jewels back from pirates Cook, Line and Sinker. Game show for children(n)

programme.title: Andy's Prehistoric Adventures

programme.title: Maddie's Do You Know?
programme.desc: It's bedtime and Maddie is finding out about different things we sleep on and in. At a campsite she uses a special thermal camera to show how the layers in a sleeping bag work by trapping warm air inside to keep us warm(n)

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: The Essam family visit the Brooklyn-Bells. Jess hopes their well-behaved dog, Baxter, will teach Waffle how to be good, but Waffle has other ideas(n)

programme.title: Topsy and Tim

programme.title: My World Kitchen

programme.title: Old Jack's Boat

programme.title: Down on the Farm

programme.title: Where in the World?
programme.desc: In Jordan, eight-year-old Hamza makes lunch with his mum, in Vietnam, Truc, who is seven, makes paper butterflies at school and wins a relay race, and in Italy, seven-year-old Mario and his brother and sister spend the day at their grandma's house, dressing up and eating ice-cream(n)

programme.title: Defenders UK

programme.desc: Two teams are challenged to scour an antiques fair and pick up bargains to sell for profit at auction(n)

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather

programme.title: Doctors

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Seventeen contenders start this edition of the quiz, but how many of them will survive to face the £10,000 Question? Hosted by Rick Edwards(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton accompanies a couple on a North Yorkshire house hunt. They are calling time on running a guest house in the city and have a £650 000 budget to find a rural family home with business potential. Whilst in the county, Alistair also pays a visit to a heritage train line and helps the team to get their impressive locomotives fired up for the start of the tourist season(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Oxfordshire mum Sarah is after a space where she can relax and unwind after a full-on day minding two young children, but is also aware that the garden also needs to work as a play space for the youngsters. programme.title: Antiques Road Trip

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show

programme.title: Iolo's Street Life
programme.desc: Iolo Williams explores the wild side of towns and finds birds and animals that have adapted to life on the street. In the opening programme, he stumbles on tawny owls in residence in an Abertillery chimney a thriving colony of rats in the centre of Swansea and a fox family living in an allotment in Cardiff. Iolo also finds the best fed swans in Wales, while a heron visits a Llanberis garden for his daily diet of junk food(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Burnley defender James Tarkowski, Great Britain sprinter Asha Philip, IPC World Championships long jump champion Stef Reid and Olympic silver medal-winning swimmer Michael Jamieson join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Lisa supports Mel as she prepares for Hunter's funeral. Over at the Mitchells', however, Phil is adamant that none of his family will attend, leaving Louise conflicted - and things only get worse when Phil realises that Jay went behind his back to help Mel. Meanwhile, Tiffany and Keegan agree to take things slowly, while Patrick is determined to talk to Ted about making a pass at Sheree - only to realise he misses Joyce(n)

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: The latest battle for a semi-final place reaches its climax as the four celebrities are tasked to cook a faultless two-course meal that will not only be judged by John Torode and Gregg Wallace but also by former contenders Lisa Faulkner, Kimberly Wyatt and Adrian Edmondson. Only the best two contestants can come one step closer to winning the title of Celebrity MasterChef 2019. For those who don't make the grade, the journey ends here(n)

programme.title: Scarborough
programme.desc: On the day of Mrs Bookham's funeral, a blazing row about the contents of the deceased's handbag sees Lisa Marie storm out telling Geraldine she can 'stick her job'. At the arcade Mike receives a visit from a disgruntled Tony Peroni who wants to call in his debt, while suspicions mounts about Bigsy's involvement in Mr Ferris's burglary. Karen has doubts about her relationship with Mike and during a heart to heart with Lisa Marie confesses that there was a lot more to the kiss between Mike and Hayley Cox. Comedy, starring Stephanie Cole, Maggie Ollerenshaw, Catherine Tyldesley and Jason Manford(n)

programme.title: Tudur's TV Flashback
programme.desc: Comedian Tudur Owen references clips from half a century and more of Welsh television as he sets out on a quest to discover the truth about rugby. It's a story of vodka, Groggs and adventures away, full of lively characters from the nation's sporting past.(n)

programme.title: Room 101 - Extra Storage
programme.desc: Extended edition of the comedy panel show presented by Frank Skinner, with Richard Ayoade, Alun Cochrane and Gabby Logan competing to have their pet hates consigned to Room 101, with topics including people who laugh out loud when reading a book, fun and half and half scarves(n)

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: An elderly amateur sleuth who was a friend of Danny's is murdered, and he ends up embroiled in the case when his photo is found at the scene. However, the cold case copper becomes convinced that the secret to uncovering her real killer lies within the last case she was working on. Picking up where she left off, he travels to the village of Minchampton, where a woman was said to have been murdered by a local lad 25 years previously. Meanwhile, Sasha is left to cope with the investigating officer on the modern-day Miss Marple's murder - an overbearing contemporary from her Hendon training, by the name of DCI Grace Mackie - and Steve lets Gerry stay with him when he is evicted, but soon comes to regret the act of kindness(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Jon Kay and Naga Munchetty present national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live

programme.title: Down the Social with Owen Money
programme.desc: Comedian Owen Money visits Merthyr Borough Ex-Servicemen's Club, the place where he first performed in public aged 16. The town has produced many champion boxers through the years and Owen discovers the gym above the club continuing that tradition. Currently, it's where Welsh Commonwealth Games boxer Lynsey Holdaway trains. As Owen sets about re-staging his first gig at the club with familiar faces in the crowd, there is a shocking turn of events(n)

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests review the week's headlines and look ahead to forthcoming fixtures, including Chelsea v Liverpool and Manchester City v Watford in the Premier League(n)

programme.title: The Super League Show

programme.title: Money for Nothing

While furniture designer Rupert Blanchard is hoping he can create a top end piece from a pair of metal bench ends, Sarah takes on the challenge of restyling a fashion mannequin(n)

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland(n)

The second round sees them tackling general knowledge questions in a bid to carry off the commemorative trophy(n)

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman invite another selection of famous faces to take part in the unorthodox general knowledge quiz. As always, the challengers are tasked with coming up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public. This time, all the contestants come from the world of journalism, as Owen Jones, Paris Lees, Kay Burley, Adam Boulton, Carole Malone, Quentin Letts, Amol Rajan and Victoria Derbyshire take part(n)

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman present the first live episode of this year's series, with a line-up featuring the likes of Alex Scott, Emma Barton and James Cracknell. Having had only two weeks to rehearse their first routine, the 15 couples take to the ballroom floor for the first time. The celebrities are dancing to impress the viewers and the four judges, new judge Motsi Mabuse, Bruno Tonioli, Craig Revel Horwood and Head Judge Shirley Ballas(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Hospital drama with the staff of Holby's accident and emergency department. Taking his own advice with reassurance from Ruby and Jan, Iain decides to join the HEMS team. Meanwhile, Rash's career is in jeopardy when he gets blamed for a mistake made by Mason, and Ethan's fascination with Effie deepens(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Manchester City v Watford at the Etihad Stadium and Burnley v Norwich City at Turf Moor. This was the first meeting between the Citizens and Hornets since the FA Cup final in May, when Pep Guardiola's men ran riot in a thumping 6-0 victory at Wembley Stadium. That was City's 11th successive victory over Watford, a record stretching back to April 2007. Plus, action from Everton v Sheffield United at Goodison Park, Leicester City v Tottenham Hotspur at the King Power Stadium, Newcastle United v Brighton & Hove Albion at St James' Park, and Southampton v Bournemouth, which took place on Friday night at St Mary's Stadium(n)

programme.title: The Rap Game UK
programme.desc: In the first challenge, online influencers offer the remaining artists their honest feedback, with online presence, lyrics, personalities and sense of style all under the microscope. For the second challenge, the contestants are paired-up with an esteemed video director to shoot a music video(n)

programme.title: The NFL Show

programme.title: Landward
programme.desc: The team returns with stories from the heart of the Scottish countryside. Dougie Vipond asks if Scottish livestock farming can survive climate change, while Anne Lundon visits the farmer's fields being transformed into a final resting place. Euan McIlwraith sails out from Mull in search of the secretive basking shark and Arlene Stuart discovers the fruitful history of apple growing on Clydeside. Plus, Nick Nairn parks the Landward food van in Moffat, where his ingredients include air-dried jerky, Scottish-grown chillis and prime Galloway beef(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Burnley defender James Tarkowski, Great Britain sprinter Asha Philip, IPC World Championships long jump champion Stef Reid and Olympic silver medal-winning swimmer Michael Jamieson join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Lisa supports Mel as she prepares for Hunter's funeral. Over at the Mitchells', however, Phil is adamant that none of his family will attend, leaving Louise conflicted - and things only get worse when Phil realises that Jay went behind his back to help Mel. Meanwhile, Tiffany and Keegan agree to take things slowly, while Patrick is determined to talk to Ted about making a pass at Sheree - only to realise he misses Joyce(n)

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: The latest battle for a semi-final place reaches its climax as the four celebrities are tasked to cook a faultless two-course meal that will not only be judged by John Torode and Gregg Wallace but also by former contenders Lisa Faulkner, Kimberly Wyatt and Adrian Edmondson. Only the best two contestants can come one step closer to winning the title of Celebrity MasterChef 2019. For those who don't make the grade, the journey ends here(n)

programme.title: Scarborough
programme.desc: On the day of Mrs Bookham's funeral, a blazing row about the contents of the deceased's handbag sees Lisa Marie storm out telling Geraldine she can 'stick her job'. At the arcade Mike receives a visit from a disgruntled Tony Peroni who wants to call in his debt, while suspicions mounts about Bigsy's involvement in Mr Ferris's burglary. Karen has doubts about her relationship with Mike and during a heart to heart with Lisa Marie confesses that there was a lot more to the kiss between Mike and Hayley Cox. Comedy, starring Stephanie Cole, Maggie Ollerenshaw, Catherine Tyldesley and Jason Manford(n)

programme.title: Room 101 - Extra Storage
programme.desc: Extended edition of the comedy panel show presented by Frank Skinner, with Richard Ayoade, Alun Cochrane and Gabby Logan competing to have their pet hates consigned to Room 101, with topics including people who laugh out loud when reading a book, fun and half and half scarves(n)

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: An elderly amateur sleuth who was a friend of Danny's is murdered, and he ends up embroiled in the case when his photo is found at the scene. However, the cold case copper becomes convinced that the secret to uncovering her real killer lies within the last case she was working on. Picking up where she left off, he travels to the village of Minchampton, where a woman was said to have been murdered by a local lad 25 years previously. Meanwhile, Sasha is left to cope with the investigating officer on the modern-day Miss Marple's murder - an overbearing contemporary from her Hendon training, by the name of DCI Grace Mackie - and Steve lets Gerry stay with him when he is evicted, but soon comes to regret the act of kindness(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Jon Kay and Naga Munchetty present national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Glynn Purnell is joined by chefs Sarit Packer, Itamar Srulovich and Bryn Williams. Plus, special guest Iain Stirling chooses his food heaven or food hell(n)

programme.title: Mary Berry's Quick Cooking
programme.desc: Mary boards Royal Navy destroyer HMS Defender, helping the team in the galley with the mammoth task of preparing, cooking and serving 240 meals from scratch in just 75 minutes. In her own kitchen, she creates dishes inspired by her experience at sea, including a crumble fish pie, special cauliflower soup and mini sausage rolls(n)

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests review the week's headlines and look ahead to forthcoming fixtures, including Chelsea v Liverpool and Manchester City v Watford in the Premier League(n)

programme.title: The Super League Show
programme.desc: Tanya Arnold presents action from Warrington Wolves v Castleford Tigers in the Elimination Final and Wigan Warriors v Salford Red Devils in the Qualifying Final. With commentary by Dave Woods(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross presents a double edition of the antiques hunting show from the East of England Showground in Peterborough, and finds time to visit Blakesley Hall in Birmingham(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: After a successful day of salvaging at the recycling centre in Altrincham, Greater Manchester, upcycling expert Sarah Moore tasks carpenter Norman Wilkinson with reimagining an old wall cabinet. While furniture designer Rupert Blanchard is hoping he can create a top end piece from a pair of metal bench ends, Sarah takes on the challenge of restyling a fashion mannequin(n)

programme.title: Sportscene Results
programme.desc: David Currie presents the classified results and a round-up of the afternoon's main football news. Plus, a look back at the week's talking points(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys invites comedian Elis James, Love Island's Montana Brown, actress Madeline Smith and Casualty star George Rainsford to take to the black chair to be put to the test on their chosen specialist subjects, which are the Welsh football team, Desperate Housewives, Kew Gardens and the Back to the Future trilogy. The second round sees them tackling general knowledge questions in a bid to carry off the commemorative trophy(n)

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman invite another selection of famous faces to take part in the unorthodox general knowledge quiz. As always, the challengers are tasked with coming up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public. This time, all the contestants come from the world of journalism, as Owen Jones, Paris Lees, Kay Burley, Adam Boulton, Carole Malone, Quentin Letts, Amol Rajan and Victoria Derbyshire take part(n)

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman present the first live episode of this year's series, with a line-up featuring the likes of Alex Scott, Emma Barton and James Cracknell. Having had only two weeks to rehearse their first routine, the 15 couples take to the ballroom floor for the first time. The celebrities are dancing to impress the viewers and the four judges, new judge Motsi Mabuse, Bruno Tonioli, Craig Revel Horwood and Head Judge Shirley Ballas(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Hospital drama with the staff of Holby's accident and emergency department. Taking his own advice with reassurance from Ruby and Jan, Iain decides to join the HEMS team. Meanwhile, Rash's career is in jeopardy when he gets blamed for a mistake made by Mason, and Ethan's fascination with Effie deepens(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Manchester City v Watford at the Etihad Stadium and Burnley v Norwich City at Turf Moor. This was the first meeting between the Citizens and Hornets since the FA Cup final in May, when Pep Guardiola's men ran riot in a thumping 6-0 victory at Wembley Stadium. That was City's 11th successive victory over Watford, a record stretching back to April 2007. Plus, action from Everton v Sheffield United at Goodison Park, Leicester City v Tottenham Hotspur at the King Power Stadium, Newcastle United v Brighton & Hove Albion at St James' Park, and Southampton v Bournemouth, which took place on Friday night at St Mary's Stadium(n)

programme.title: The Rap Game UK
programme.desc: In the first challenge, online influencers offer the remaining artists their honest feedback, with online presence, lyrics, personalities and sense of style all under the microscope. For the second challenge, the contestants are paired-up with an esteemed video director to shoot a music video(n)

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by former players Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to present highlights of Thursday night's game - Jacksonville Jaguars v Tennessee Titans at TIAA Bank Field(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Chris Mason presents a round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Manchester City v Watford at the Etihad Stadium and Burnley v Norwich City at Turf Moor. programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00(n)

programme.title: Sunday Politics Scotland
programme.desc: The latest political news and debate, with reports from around Scotland. Presented by Gordon Brewer(n)

programme.title: Sunday Morning Live
programme.desc: Topical debate covering religious, moral and ethical issues, with guest panellists and contributions from viewers. Presented by Sean Fletcher and Ria Hebden(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under Revisited
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman catches up with the Mungur family, who decided to spend a trial week living in Brisbane, Australia 12 months earlier, to see whether they made the move on a permanent basis(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Philip Serrell and Nick Hall lend their antiques expertise to two teams in Lewes, East Sussex, as they compete to make a profit at auction. Presented by Anita Manning(n)

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: The Rev Kate Bottley visits Norwich Cathedral to see how a 50ft helter-skelter is attracting families, allowing them to see the ceiling sculptures and bringing the Bible to life(n)

programme.title: Points of View
programme.desc: Viewers' opinions on BBC shows and behind-the-scenes reports on new programmes(n)

programme.title: Lifeline
programme.desc: Gaby Roslin presents a lifeline appeal on behalf of WellChild - a charity helping to get seriously ill children and young people out of hospital and home to their families(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell explore a one-bedroom flat high on potential in London's Streatham, a coastal bungalow in Cumbria and a semi-detached property near Derby with scope for an extension. The hosts see what each went for at auction and talk to the new owners' about their plans for their aquisitions(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin is in Norfolk to help a couple with an £800,000 budget buy their first property together, and rides on a narrow gauge steam train when he meets the enthusiasts keeping the Bure Valley Railway running(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers are tasked with transforming a long, thin garden in Northampton, and decide to create a multi-functional space the whole family can enjoy(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jay Blades saves a battered suitcase, a wingback chair and a Formica table top from the recycling centre, and tries to transform them with the help of Daniel Heath and Ray Clarke(n)

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present a musical theatre edition of the general knowledge quiz, in which four teams try to come up with the answers that nobody else could think of. Featuring Ray Quinn, Joanne Clifton, Ben Forster, Kerry Ellis, Jenna Russell, Hannah Waddingham, Duncan James and Jade Ewen(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: The programme plays host to One Man and His Dog 2019, as the best shepherds from across the British Isles descend on the Scottish Borders to find out which nation has what it takes to claim this coveted title. Matt Baker and Helen Skelton present proceedings from Bowhill House, as the best youngsters and senior shepherds from England, Wales, Ireland and Scotland are teamed up in a bid to win the trophy for their country(n)

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce heads to the waterfront beside the V&A Dundee design museum, on the east coast of Scotland. She explores the fascinating story behind the landmark museum and the treasures it houses. Among the discoveries are a valuable bowl found at a car-boot sale for just £3, a set of rare SAS medals and a Vivienne Westwood jacket(n)

programme.title: Peaky Blinders
programme.desc: News of Tommy's activities has reached Winston Churchill. At a family meeting, Tommy lays out his plans for Mosley, which will take place at a rally Sir Oswald is leading. Has he underestimated his opponent? Tommy is surprised by another strategy coming from an unexpected corner, and he also has his own secrets to reveal. Drama, starring Cillian Murphy, Sam Claflin, Paul Anderson, Helen McCrory and Cosmo Jarvis(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman presents action from today's Premier League fixtures, including Chelsea v Liverpool at Stamford Bridge, as the Blues looked to avenge last season's 2-1 defeat here. Plus, Crystal Palace v Wolverhampton Wanderers at Selhurst Park, West Ham United v Manchester United at London Stadium, Arsenal v Aston Villa at Emirates Stadium, and a round-up of yesterday's goals(n)

programme.title: Sportscene
programme.desc: Jonathan Sutherland is joined by Michael Stewart and Steven Thompson to present action from the weekend's Premiership matches, including Hibernian v Hearts, Celtic v Kilmarnock and St Johnstone v Rangers. Plus, the draw for the second round of the Scottish Cup(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Crimewatch Roadshow
programme.desc: Michelle Ackerley visits the Metropolitan Police's Mounted Unit, who are celebrating 100 years at their Surrey training centre. Back in the studio, Rav Wilding has the latest appeals for viewers' help(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: An update on an unusual project in Capel Le Ferne in Kent that Lucy Alexander first visited in 2009. Plus, a mid-terrace house in Liverpool and a cottage in Plaistow, east London(n)

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: The enforcement officers investigate the tragic death of a Red Arrows pilot, and they smash a puppy-smuggling operation. A supermarket is fined £8million after a massive fuel leak at one of its petrol stations has a devastating impact on the local community and environment.(n)

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: Nick Knowles tells the story of a paraglider hanging by a thread in a forest after his canopy collapses. A dash cam captures the moment three cars collide on a mountain road, and a bullock on the pitch nearly hits a village cricket team for six(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Two teams are challenged to scour an antiques fair and pick up bargains to sell for profit at auction(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: As Emma's dreams escalate, Zara has a radical idea about how to stop them, and Becky puts Daniel on the spot. Ayesha deals with the fallout when an angry patient ignores the warning signs that his health is in danger(n)

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Seventeen contenders start this edition of the quiz, but how many of them will survive to face the £10,000 Question? Hosted by Rick Edwards(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton accompanies a couple on a North Yorkshire house hunt. They are calling time on running a guest house in the city and have a £650 000 budget to find a rural family home with business potential. Whilst in the county, Alistair also pays a visit to a heritage train line and helps the team to get their impressive locomotives fired up for the start of the tourist season(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Oxfordshire mum Sarah is after a space where she can relax and unwind after a full-on day minding two young children, but is also aware that the garden also needs to work as a play space for the youngsters. Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich head to Bicester to assist Sarah and partner Adam, who need a garden the whole family can play and relax in(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Izzie Balmer and James Braxton tour Izzie's native Derbyshire on the final leg, where she tempts the gourmand with some local delicacies. There's a blast from the past as James is carried away by the news from the 1780s while Izzie follows in the tracks of Winston Churchill. Then, it's onwards to auction in Lincoln(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: The usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: The Rich brothers and Arit Anderson travel to Shefford in Bedfordshire, where they meet a couple who claim their garden is basic and plain. Duncan would like it to be modern, but also contain elements of a rustic, cottage garden. Both he and Karen like the idea of a curved patio and wish to avoid decking. Will the couple choose the Rich brothers proposed design, complete with bespoke veg beds, pair of curved hedges and paved courtyard, or will Arit's design that offers a new circular lawn, choice of paved seating areas and a vertical waterfall be more to their taste?(n)
