programme.desc: Po is excited to meet a famous wrestler - but finds he is not quite as heroic as he appears(n)

programme.title: An Rud As Fheàrr Leam
programme.desc: Lucas from Lewis talks about his favourite hobbies, including fishing, crofting and cycling(n)

programme.title: Sràid nan Sgread (Scream Street)
programme.desc: Letting curiosity get the better of him, Luke finds danger in a new house that has popped up on the street(n)

programme.title: Seonaidh (Shaun the Sheep)
programme.desc: Bitzer's over-officious management style becomes too much for the sheep who decide he must chill out. Bitzer's tasks then take on a new purpose - for everyone to have some fun(n)

programme.title: Sealgairean Spòrsail (History Hunters)
programme.desc: The Maya tribe, whose members lived deep in the rainforests of Central America in countries like Mexico, Guatemala and Belize(n)

programme.title: An Là (News)
programme.desc: Local, national and international news reports(n)

programme.title: Seachd Là (News Review)
programme.desc: Local, national and international news reports(n)

programme.title: Alleluia! (Spiritual Music & Verse)
programme.desc: A compilation show featuring a selection of hymns and psalms, including performances by Govan Gaelic Choir, Norrie MacIver, Sineag MacIntyre and Kathleen Graham(n)

programme.title: An Lot (The Croft)
programme.desc: It's a very special time for Sweeny on the croft as he welcomes the first calf to be born on the croft since his grandfather was a crofter(n)

programme.title: Druthag na Hearadh (A Little Drop of Harris)
programme.desc: With visitor numbers exceeding all expectations, and sales of Harris gin hugely surpassing initial forecasts, the team members work hard to keep up. However, it's not all taking place inside the distillery, Peter the Retail Manager is curious about where peat, used in the whisky making, comes from. Domhnall, one of the distillers, happily obliges and takes him out to the site of his grandfather's peat banks. As the distillate has been tweaked and optimised, the first private cask is filled by Anderson Bakewell, founder and chairman of the distillery, and in what seems like the blink of an eye the first year anniversary is upon them(n)

programme.title: Mathair a' Chinn Suidhe - Trump's Mother
programme.desc: The life story of the little-known Scottish mother of US President Donald Trump, Mary Anne MacLeod, a Gaelic-speaking Scottish island immigrant(n)

programme.title: Manran aig Barrowland
programme.desc: A concert from Mànran & Trail West recorded on St Andrew's Day, 2018. The bands play in front of the 2,000 music fans who packed this concert at the fabled Barrowland Ballroom(n)

programme.title: The Flatey Enigma
programme.desc: Johanna is dragged into the murder investigation, while Brynjar has Snorri taken into care(n)

programme.title: Dhan Uisge
programme.desc: Calum Maclean explores one of the biggest and deepest lochs in Scotland - Loch Morar(n)

programme.title: Good Morning Britain
programme.desc: Morning magazine featuring a lively mix of news and current affairs, plus health, entertainment and lifestyle features(n)

programme.title: Lorraine
programme.desc: Entertainment, current affairs and fashion news, as well as showbiz stories and celebrity gossip. Presented by Lorraine Kelly(n)

programme.title: Judge Rinder
programme.desc: Cameras follow criminal barrister Robert Rinder as he takes on real-life cases in a studio courtroom(n)

programme.title: Rugby World Cup 2019 Live
programme.desc: Mark Pougatch is joined in the studio by Jonny Wilkinson, Brian O'Driscoll and Sam Warburton to present coverage of the opening ceremony from Tokyo Stadium, as the tournament is hosted in Asia for the first time. With commentary by Miles Harrison(n)

programme.title: Rugby World Cup 2019 Live
programme.desc: Japan v Russia (Kick-off 11.45am). Mark Pougatch presents coverage of the opening match of the tournament, as the Pool A sides meet at Tokyo Stadium. Although they would ultimately miss out on progressing to the group stage, the hosts were one of the surprise packages in the last staging of the competition four years ago, producing one of the biggest shocks in World Cup history when they overcame South Africa 34-32 in their opener. They will be looking for another tournament to remember on home soil and start as favourites against the Russians, whose only previous appearance in a finals was in 2011, when they lost all four pool fixtures. With analysis from Jonny Wilkinson, Brian O'Driscoll and Sam Warburton, reports by Jill Douglas and Bryan Habana and commentary from Miles Harrison, Shane Williams and Jamie Roberts(n)

programme.title: James Martin's French Adventure
programme.desc: The chef travels through Charente in southwestern France, visiting a distillery in the Cognac region and meeting a miller who still uses traditional methods to make award-winning brioche loaves. He also creates a salad incorporating some of the region's best produce, including the brioche(n)

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Warwick Davis which sees a team of five friends from Southampton compete to answer questions about top 10 lists from the realms of pop culture and general knowledge, then try to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money(n)

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot(n)

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four contestants who have never met before pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. Will, Steve, Marilyn and Becky work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Zak's stance frustrates Mandy, Kim is forced to let Jamie down, and Jai struggles to remain positive(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: The neighbours gather for Ed's birthday party, and Gary discovers what Derek has been up to in his absence. Meanwhile, a morose Daniel drowns his sorrows(n)

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Part two of two. Despite encountering suspicion from new Chief Superintendent Joe Moody, it seems Lewis's decision to come out of retirement could not have come at a better moment for Oxfordshire Police. The murder of the young avant-garde artist Talika Desai is linked to the unidentified human remains discovered in the well, and the subsequent investigation takes Lewis, Hathaway and the team deep into a world of drugs, social media, alternative art and the growing Eastern European homeless community. Crime drama, starring Kevin Whately, Laurence Fox and Steve Toussaint(n)

programme.title: Lethal Weapon
programme.desc: While Cole gets into the holiday spirit for his first Christmas with Maya, Murtaugh hopes he can convince RJ to come back home from Costa Rica for the holidays. At the precinct, Cole and Murtaugh are saddled with a complicated burglary and home invasion case. Meanwhile, Avery's holidays are a bit blue, and Bailey is still trying to figure out her new partner. Action drama, starring Damon Wayans and Seann William Scott(n)

programme.title: The Jonathan Ross Show
programme.desc: The host is joined for the launch of a new series by Downton Abbey stars Elizabeth McGovern and Michelle Dockery, who discuss reviving the period drama for a new movie. Other guests include Stephen Fry, Craig David and Martin Freeman, currently starring in true-life crime drama A Confession. Plus, Charli XCX and Christine and The Queens perform together in the studio(n)

programme.title: Rugby World Cup 2019 Highlights
programme.desc: Japan v Russia. Jill Douglas presents action from the opening match of the tournament, as the Pool A teams met at Tokyo Stadium. With tough tests to come against Ireland, Scotland and Samoa, both sides will have been desperate to get off to a winning start. With commentary from Miles Harrison, Shane Williams and Jamie Roberts(n)

programme.title: Ideal World
programme.desc: Home shopping(n)

programme.title: Ideal World
programme.desc: Home shopping(n)

programme.title: The Level
programme.desc: Mistrustful of her colleagues, Nancy desperately needs an ally - but cannot open up to Hayley for fear of putting them both in danger. When the police make a public appeal hoping to shake out the missing witness, Nancy is unexpectedly forced into the spotlight, leaving her exposed and bringing an old enemy out of the woodwork - Hayley's ex-boyfriend. Meanwhile, the murder investigation focuses on Frank Le Saux's accountant Theo Kettler, whom a witness links to a Newhaven garage suspected of drugs trafficking. Drama, starring Karla Crome, Laura Haddock and Joe Absolom(n)

programme.title: ITV Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service(n)

programme.title: Rugby World Cup 2019 Live
programme.desc: Australia v Fiji (Kick-off 5.45am). Craig Doyle presents coverage of the opening Pool D encounter, which comes from Sapporo Dome in Japan. Australia demonstrated their credentials with a 47-26 win over reigning champions New Zealand last month, but will not be underestimating the Fijians, who have produced a number of upsets in past World Cups. With analysis from Michael Lynagh, Lawrence Dallaglio and Maggie Alphonsi, and commentary by Nick Mullins, Ugo Monye and Ben Kay(n)

programme.title: Rugby World Cup 2019 Live
programme.desc: France v Argentina (Kick-off 8.15am). Craig Doyle presents coverage of the opening contest in Pool C, which takes place at Tokyo Stadium. With analysis by Jonny Wilkinson, Paul O'Connell and Lawrence Dallaglio, and commentary from Martin Gillingham and Shane Williams(n)

programme.title: Rugby World Cup 2019 Live
programme.desc: New Zealand v South Africa (Kick-off 10.45am). Mark Pougatch presents all the action from the opening game in Pool B, which comes from International Stadium Yokohama in Kanagawa Prefecture, Yokohama City. The sides met in the semi-finals of this tournament four years ago, when New Zealand recorded a narrow 20-18 victory en route to retaining the title. The last meeting between the sides was a 16-16 draw two months ago, and the three matches before that were all decided by two points or less, so another closely fought contest is anticipated today. With analysis by Jonny Wilkinson, Sean Fitzpatrick and Paul O'Connell, reports by Jill Douglas and Bryan Habana, and commentary from Miles Harrison, Scott Hastings and Jamie Roberts(n)

programme.title: The Witches (1990)
programme.desc: A boy goes on holiday with his grandmother to a seaside hotel hosting a convention of witches, who have come together to hear their leader's plan to turn all the children in England into mice. When the chief hag discovers the young lad eavesdropping at their meeting, she decides to make him one of her first victims. Children's fantasy directed by Nicolas Roeg, based on the book by Roald Dahl, and starring Anjelica Huston, Mai Zetterling, Jasen Fisher, Jane Horrocks, Rowan Atkinson and Brenda Blethyn(n)

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Warwick Davis hosts the quiz, which sees a family from Leeds competing to answer questions about top 10 lists from the realms of popular culture and general knowledge. They then try to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money(n)

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot(n)

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill applies his unique form of slapstick satire to another selection of home-video blunders, submitted for approval by viewers. This time, a toddler has an explosive encounter with a cake, and a pole-dancer wrecks a kitchen(n)

programme.title: Catchphrase
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts a celebrity edition of the game show, asking contestants Richard Osman, Gregg Wallace and Penny Lancaster to 'see it and say it' as they try to guess the familiar phrases hidden in animated clues. After the first elimination, the two remaining players battle to reach the Super Catchphrase round, where one of them could win £50,000 for the charity of their choice(n)

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents a Coronation Street edition of the quiz, as celebrity contestants Shelley King, Daniel Brocklebank, Kym Marsh and Jack P Shepherd pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught(n)

programme.title: Britain's Got Talent: The Champions
programme.desc: Ant and Dec present a special version of the talent show, in which stars from the past 13 series of Britain's Got Talent compete against winners from versions of the programme from around the world to impress the viewers and judges Simon Cowell, Amanda Holden, Alesha Dixon and David Walliams. Each week, two acts will make it through to the grand finale. The first is a Golden Buzzer act chosen by one of the judges, or Ant and Dec, while the second is voted through by superfans in the arena audience(n)

programme.title: The Jonathan Ross Show
programme.desc: The host is joined in the studio for a chat by another group of celebrity guests. Dropping by this week are Sean Bean, Brassic star Michelle Keegan, comedian Rose Matafeo, and Liam Gallagher, who will also be performing live(n)

programme.title: Rugby World Cup 2019 Highlights
programme.desc: Action from the opening round of fixtures in Pools D, C and B - Australia v Fiji in Sapporo, France v Argentina in Tokyo and New Zealand v South Africa in Yokohama City. Jill Douglas presents, with commentary from Scott Hastings and Jamie Roberts(n)

programme.title: Football Genius
programme.desc: Stand-up comedian Tim Vine hosts the comedy quiz, in which two teams of football supporters, managed by The Chase's Paul Sinha and Olympic Gold medallist Sam Quek, play against each other in a game of virtual football by answering football-related questions. This match features two of the biggest clubs in English football as Paul's team of Arsenal fans go head to head with Sam's Liverpool side for the Football Genius trophy(n)

programme.title: Who's Doing the Dishes?
programme.desc: Anthea Turner plays mystery host as she throws a dinner party for four strangers at her home. All they have to do is guess who has been cooking for them to win a cash prize and leave the TV presenter doing his own washing up. But if she manages to keep her identity intact, they'll be the ones pulling on the Marigolds. Presented by Brian McFadden(n)

programme.title: ITV Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service(n)

programme.title: Rugby World Cup 2019 Live
programme.desc: Italy v Namibia (Kick-off 6.15am). Craig Doyle presents coverage of the Pool B fixture, which takes place at Hanazono Rugby Stadium in Osaka Prefecture, Higashiosaka City. With analysis from Brian O'Driscoll, Ian McGeechan and Paul O'Connell, and commentary by Simon Ward and Danielle Waterman(n)

programme.title: Rugby World Cup 2019 Live
programme.desc: Ireland v Scotland (Kick-off 8.45am). Craig Doyle presents coverage of the Pool A match from International Stadium Yokohama, which is the only meeting between two home nations in the pool stage. With analysis from Brian O'Driscoll, Paul O'Connell, Ian McGeechan and Jim Hamilton, reports by Jill Douglas and Bryan Habana, and commentary from Miles Harrison, Alan Quinlan and Scott Hastings(n)

programme.title: Rugby World Cup 2019 Live
programme.desc: England v Tonga (Kick-off 11.15am). Mark Pougatch presents coverage of both teams' opening Pool C match, which comes from Sapporo Dome. Eddie Jones' men recorded some impressive results in warm-up matches against Wales and Ireland, and will be expecting nothing less than a comfortable victory against a Tongan side that lost 92-7 to New Zealand earlier this month. However, the World Cup has sprung up a number of shock results over the year, and England will be eager to avoid complacency with tougher fixtures on paper to follow against France and Argentina. With analysis from Jonny Wilkinson, Clive Woodward and Lawrence Dallaglio, reports from David Flatman, and commentary by Nick Mullins, Ben Kay and Ugo Monye(n)

programme.title: Paul O'Grady's Little Heroes
programme.desc: Paul heads to the mental health unit, where he joins 10-year-old Tourette's sufferer Oscar at a group therapy session where the youngster hopes to buuild up his confidence. The presenter also meets 13-year-old Lola, who has been struck down by a rare muscle-weakening auto-immune disease that affects just one in a million people, and 15-year old Joe, who has arrived for major surgery to repair a faulty heart valve(n)

programme.title: Britain's Got Talent: The Champions
programme.desc: Ant and Dec present a special version of the talent show, in which stars from the past 13 series of Britain's Got Talent compete against winners from versions of the programme from around the world to impress the viewers and judges Simon Cowell, Amanda Holden, Alesha Dixon and David Walliams. Each week, two acts will make it through to the grand finale. The first is a Golden Buzzer act chosen by one of the judges, or Ant and Dec, while the second is voted through by superfans in the arena audience(n)

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as contestants Ben, Pat, Vicki and Yvonne pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser. Answering general knowledge questions, they each add as much money as they dare to the jackpot - but anyone caught by the Chaser is out for good, leaving fewer players to team up and play in the final chase(n)

programme.title: Tipping Point: Lucky Stars
programme.desc: Ben Shephard invites music legend Tito Jackson, actress Faye Brookes (Corrie's Kate Connor) and Good Morning Britain weather presenter Laura Tobin to take part in a special edition of the quiz show, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the better their chances of collecting the £20,000 prize for their selected charity(n)

programme.title: 5 Gold Rings
programme.desc: A married couple and two sisters take part in the game show, competing to answer visual questions by placing rings on an LED floor in five increasingly difficult stages, with a potential jackpot of more than £50,000 up for grabs. Hosted by Phillip Schofield(n)

programme.title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson hosts the big-prize quiz in which another line-up of contestants answer questions that could change their lives - with the lifelines Ask the Audience, Phone a Friend, 50-50 and Ask the Host available to them if they get stuck(n)

programme.title: Sanditon
programme.desc: Tensions between Tom and his labourers erupt during the Sanditon cricket match. Charlotte and Sidney continue to clash, resulting in Miss Lambe being placed in terrible danger, while Lord Babington offers Esther a glimpse of a better future. Period drama, starring Rose Williams, Theo James, Kris Marshall, Crystal Clarke and Charlotte Spencer(n)

programme.title: Billy Connolly's Great American Trail
programme.desc: Billy's final leg begins in Virginia on the trail of bootleggers dealing in moonshine before he hears the story of the amazing woman who led hundreds of slaves to their freedom. He stops off at a farm where he encounters bizarre heads of all the US presidents, learns about the opioid crisis - the biggest epidemic in American history - and visits the backroom of flamboyant designer Manuel Cuuevas, who was responsible for dressing rock stars including Elvis, Cher and the Beatles. The comedian's tour finally ends in Nashville at the home of country music, the Grand Ole Opry(n)

programme.title: Rugby World Cup 2019 Highlights
programme.desc: Jill Douglas present action from the latest pool fixtures in Japan, featuring Ireland v Scotland, England v Tonga and Italy v Namibia. With commentary from Nick Mullins, Miles Harrison, Simon Ward, Ugo Monye, Ben Kay, Alan Quinlan, Scott Hastings and Danielle Waterman(n)

programme.desc: The host is joined in the studio for a chat by another group of celebrity guests. Dropping by this week are Sean Bean, Brassic star Michelle Keegan, comedian Rose Matafeo, and Liam Gallagher, who will also be performing live(n)

programme.title: Rugby World Cup 2019 Highlights
programme.desc: Action from the opening round of fixtures in Pools D, C and B - Australia v Fiji in Sapporo, France v Argentina in Tokyo and New Zealand v South Africa in Yokohama City. Jill Douglas presents, with commentary from Scott Hastings and Jamie Roberts(n)

programme.title: Football Genius
programme.desc: Stand-up comedian Tim Vine hosts the comedy quiz, in which two teams of football supporters, managed by The Chase's Paul Sinha and Olympic Gold medallist Sam Quek, play against each other in a game of virtual football by answering football-related questions. This match features two of the biggest clubs in English football as Paul's team of Arsenal fans go head to head with Sam's Liverpool side for the Football Genius trophy(n)

programme.title: Who's Doing the Dishes?
programme.desc: Anthea Turner plays mystery host as she throws a dinner party for four strangers at her home. All they have to do is guess who has been cooking for them to win a cash prize and leave the TV presenter doing his own washing up. But if she manages to keep her identity intact, they'll be the ones pulling on the Marigolds. Presented by Brian McFadden(n)

programme.title: ITV Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service(n)

programme.title: Rugby World Cup 2019 Live
programme.desc: Ireland v Scotland (Kick-off 8.45am). Craig Doyle presents coverage of the Pool A match from International Stadium Yokohama, which is the only meeting between two home nations in the pool stage. With analysis from Brian O'Driscoll, Paul O'Connell, Ian McGeechan and Jim Hamilton, reports by Jill Douglas and Bryan Habana, and commentary from Miles Harrison, Alan Quinlan and Scott Hastings(n)

programme.title: Rugby World Cup 2019 Live
programme.desc: England v Tonga (Kick-off 11.15am). Mark Pougatch presents coverage of both teams' opening Pool C match, which comes from Sapporo Dome. Eddie Jones' men recorded some impressive results in warm-up matches against Wales and Ireland, and will be expecting nothing less than a comfortable victory against a Tongan side that lost 92-7 to New Zealand earlier this month. However, the World Cup has sprung up a number of shock results over the year, and England will be eager to avoid complacency with tougher fixtures on paper to follow against France and Argentina. With analysis from Jonny Wilkinson, Clive Woodward and Lawrence Dallaglio, reports from David Flatman, and commentary by Nick Mullins, Ben Kay and Ugo Monye(n)

programme.title: Paul O'Grady's Little Heroes
programme.desc: Paul heads to the mental health unit, where he joins 10-year-old Tourette's sufferer Oscar at a group therapy session where the youngster hopes to buuild up his confidence. The presenter also meets 13-year-old Lola, who has been struck down by a rare muscle-weakening auto-immune disease that affects just one in a million people, and 15-year old Joe, who has arrived for major surgery to repair a faulty heart valve(n)

programme.title: Britain's Got Talent: The Champions
programme.desc: Ant and Dec present a special version of the talent show, in which stars from the past 13 series of Britain's Got Talent compete against winners from versions of the programme from around the world to impress the viewers and judges Simon Cowell, Amanda Holden, Alesha Dixon and David Walliams. Each week, two acts will make it through to the grand finale. The first is a Golden Buzzer act chosen by one of the judges, or Ant and Dec, while the second is voted through by superfans in the arena audience(n)

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as contestants Ben, Pat, Vicki and Yvonne pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser. Answering general knowledge questions, they each add as much money as they dare to the jackpot - but anyone caught by the Chaser is out for good, leaving fewer players to team up and play in the final chase(n)

programme.title: Tipping Point: Lucky Stars
programme.desc: Ben Shephard invites music legend Tito Jackson, actress Faye Brookes (Corrie's Kate Connor) and Good Morning Britain weather presenter Laura Tobin to take part in a special edition of the quiz show, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the better their chances of collecting the £20,000 prize for their selected charity(n)

programme.title: 5 Gold Rings
programme.desc: A married couple and two sisters take part in the game show, competing to answer visual questions by placing rings on an LED floor in five increasingly difficult stages, with a potential jackpot of more than £50,000 up for grabs. Hosted by Phillip Schofield(n)

programme.title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson hosts the big-prize quiz in which another line-up of contestants answer questions that could change their lives - with the lifelines Ask the Audience, Phone a Friend, 50-50 and Ask the Host available to them if they get stuck(n)

programme.title: Sanditon
programme.desc: Tensions between Tom and his labourers erupt during the Sanditon cricket match. Charlotte and Sidney continue to clash, resulting in Miss Lambe being placed in terrible danger, while Lord Babington offers Esther a glimpse of a better future. Period drama, starring Rose Williams, Theo James, Kris Marshall, Crystal Clarke and Charlotte Spencer(n)

programme.title: Billy Connolly's Great American Trail
programme.desc: Billy's final leg begins in Virginia on the trail of bootleggers dealing in moonshine before he hears the story of the amazing woman who led hundreds of slaves to their freedom. He stops off at a farm where he encounters bizarre heads of all the US presidents, learns about the opioid crisis - the biggest epidemic in American history - and visits the backroom of flamboyant designer Manuel Cuuevas, who was responsible for dressing rock stars including Elvis, Cher and the Beatles. The comedian's tour finally ends in Nashville at the home of country music, the Grand Ole Opry(n)

It soon transpires that many of her companions are also travelling at his invitation and they are all in some way connected to a girl who went missing from a nun's sanctuary during World War Two, supposedly to be with her German lover, Rafiel's son Michael. programme.title: Heartbeat

Meanwhile, Merton devises a solution to Ventress's retirement worries. Rosemary Leach guest stars(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: The Aidensfield police are puzzled when it seems a face from the past has become the unlikely victim of vandalism, and suspicions are soon aroused over the reasons for his sudden return to the village. Meanwhile, Vernon encounters dangerous wildlife as he stashes his capital in Ashfordly woods, but decides to turn the incident into another money-making scheme. Geoffrey Hughes stars(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby's secret past as a spy is revealed when a former agent is killed after a cricket match between the villagers and the gentry. The dead man's wounds appear to have been inflicted by a giant claw, triggering rumours of a 'beast of Midsomer', and before long, another body turns up with similar injuries. Barnaby then realises the incidents are linked to the time the local network of spies spent in Cold War Berlin. John Nettles and Jason Hughes star, with guest appearances by Anna Massey, Peter Davison and Neil Stuke(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Midsomer University science fellow George Jeffers threatens to make public a problem with his state-of-the-art invention Kernel Logic, which he believes is potentially lethal, but American software boss Clinton Finn fears he will lose millions if the truth comes out. When a schoolteacher is killed in a hit-and-run, Barnaby suspects Jeffers was the intended target. Detective drama, guest starring David Haig and Nigel Whitmey, with John Nettles and Jason Hughes(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: Leaving behind his beloved Oxford, Morse and a reluctant Lewis set off to investigate the bizarre death of an Englishwoman who was attending a psychotherapy clinic in northern Italy. Their inquiries lead them to the vineyards and villas of Vicenza and the city of Verona - as well as a glamorous opera singer and a convicted fraudster. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star as the detective duo, with Frances Barber, Michael Kitchen, James Grout and Jolyon Baker(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Feature-length drama. While holidaying at the plush Bertram's Hotel in London, Miss Marple is presented with a fresh challenge to her deductive powers when the reading of a will leads to a double murder. Geraldine McEwan stars as the elderly spinster with a nose for a mystery, with guest appearances by Peter Davison, Mica Paris and Francesca Annis(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Wealthy industrialist Sir Reuben Astwell is murdered after making a deal to sell synthetic rubber to the Germans, leaving the dapper sleuth wondering whether the motive was commercial or political. Evidence found in his chemical factory could hold the key to the mystery - but Poirot is unsure if the victim's widow can be trusted. Starring David Suchet, with Ann Bell and Bill Wallis(n)

programme.title: Joanna Lumley's Japan
programme.desc: The actress heads to the island of Shikoku hoping to gain a better understanding of Japanese Buddhism. She then takes a bullet train to another island - Kyushu - where she finds the Henn Na Hotel, the world's first robot hotel. At Nagasaki, she visits Shiroyama Elementary school, one of the only buildings to survive the atomic bomb dropped on the city in 1945. Joanna then travels to Sakurajima, one of the country's most active volcanoes, before heading to the islands of Okinawa where one of the bloodiest battles of the Second World War was fought(n)

programme.title: The Halcyon
programme.desc: Garland asks Emma to take over running the celebrations for the 50th anniversary of the hotel, while Betsey catches the eyes of a handsome man in the bar - who is most insistent they go for a drink. programme.title: The Halcyon
programme.desc: The hotel celebrates its 50 year anniversary, but Lucian struggles to keep things in order as pressure mounts and Adil attempts to corner Toby and bring him back from the edge of doing something terrible. Garland and Lady Hamilton find a cause to unite over, while Emma is still confused about the men in her life and finally comes to a decision after talking to Betsey about love during wartime. programme.title: Heartbeat

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Sally wants to go on holiday, but Kevin knows he will not be able to get time off work. Ken starts giving Raquel French lessons, and Mike tells Jim he can start at MVB(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: Jessica checks into an Atlanta hotel to judge a writing contest, but a room mix-up means that instead of having short stories delivered to her door, she receives a parcel of surveillance photos. Angela Lansbury stars(n)

programme.title: Home Fires
programme.desc: The WI members of Great Paxford live under a shadow as the Battle of Britain looms on the horizon. Pat befriends a Czech soldier, but finds her newfound independence less enjoyable than expected. Laura faces the consequences of her affair, while Sarah receives some shocking news. Alison strikes a bargain with the police, and tragedy strikes for Frances(n)

programme.title: Home Fires
programme.desc: Laura is publicly disgraced when news of the Bowers' divorce hits the papers, with grave consequences for the surgery and her friendship with Tom. Pat is tempted by her feelings for Marek while Bob is in hospital. Steph is delighted when Stan returns home on leave, but the reunion is overshadowed by his fears of invasion. Frances discovers a secret at the reading of the will(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: As Foyle and his team search for a missing boy, their investigation leads them to a less than inviting hotel - and the discovery of the youngster's body in the nearby woods suggests one of the residents could be responsible. Milner finds his loyalty divided when Foyle is placed under house arrest, but the determined investigator vows to prove his innocence. Drama series, starring Michael Kitchen(n)

programme.title: Joe Maddison's War
programme.desc: It is 1939, and Newcastle shipyard worker Joe Maddison feels past his prime - too old to serve in the war alongside his son, who is beginning his RAF training. A few days later, when his wife leaves him for a young naval officer, Joe finds himself without purpose and in need of a new challenge - so he and best friend Harry decide to join the Home Guard. The decision is one that leads him on a journey of self-discovery and teaches him lessons in heroism, friendship and love. Feature-length drama written by Alan Plater, starring Kevin Whately, Robson Green, Derek Jacobi, Melanie Hill, Trevor Fox and John Woodvine(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Geraldine McEwan makes her last appearance as the spinster sleuth before handing over the knitting needles to Julia McKenzie. Following the death of old acquaintance Mr Rafiel, Miss Marple receives instructions drawn up prior to his demise asking her to embark on a coach tour to investigate an unspecified possible crime. It soon transpires that many of her companions are also travelling at his invitation and they are all in some way connected to a girl who went missing from a nun's sanctuary during World War Two, supposedly to be with her German lover, Rafiel's son Michael. Murder mystery, with a star-studded supporting cast including Richard E Grant, Johnny Briggs, Amanda Burton, Anne Reid, Ronni Ancona, George Cole, Will Mellor and Laura Michelle Kelly(n)

programme.title: Great Estates of Scotland
programme.desc: A tour of Inveraray castle on the shore of Loch Fyne, which was used as a location in Downton Abbey and was where American musical impresarios Lerner and Loewe wrote My Fair Lady(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Stan is attacked by hooligans during a late-night run after the pubs have shut _ so Blakey decides to give the staff a few lessons in self-defence(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Jack and Stan volunteer Olive as supervisor for the depot nursery. However, when she breaks her glasses and has to go home for a spare pair the clueless duo are left holding the fort. Comedy, starring Reg Varney, Bob Grant and Anna Karen(n)

programme.title: The Darling Buds of May
programme.desc: Part one of two. Pop befriends a pilot he rescues from his crashed plane, but suspects he is being drawn into a smuggling racket when the family is invited to a celebratory champagne night on the town. Drama, starring David Jason(n)

programme.title: The Darling Buds of May
programme.desc: Part two of two. Pop's hoard of cash causes trouble for the family when Marcus Cope resolves to stop Charley buying the hop garden at any cost. Montgomery is unhappy at naval college and the Brigadier's solution provides a challenge for Pop(n)

programme.title: The Darling Buds of May
programme.desc: Part one of two. Primrose falls for a roofing contractor, while the twins prove too much of a handful for their school, prompting a trip to view the facilities on offer at Brockhurst College. Nostalgic comedy drama, starring David Jason and Pam Ferris(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth sets out to solve the baffling murder of a Cambridge don who met with foul play shortly after deciding to change his will. Starring David Suchet, Beth Goddard and Mark Kingston(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Two couples disappear in the tiny village of Monks Barton amid rumours of witchcraft and haunted woods. As Barnaby and Jones investigate, psychic Cyrus LeVanu clashes with cleric Wallace Stone over their different methods of 'healing' the woods, while other locals seem to be caught up in a stolen antiques racket. When a body is unearthed and the death toll rises, the detectives race to catch the killer by finding a connection between the missing couples and the thefts. Anton Lesser guest stars, with John Nettles and Jason Hughes(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A prestigious golf club is the setting for murder when a player is bludgeoned to death on the 13th hole. Barnaby and Jones discover gambling, illegal money-lending and assault are rife among the snobbish members, who are also dismissive of the local villagers and only allow them on the course for a few hours a week. Before long, another body turns up, making the detectives doubly determined to catch this terror of the fairways. John Nettles and Jason Hughes star, with guest appearances by Nicholas Le Prevost, John Standing and real-life golfer Sam Torrance(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A masterpiece by landscape artist Henry Hogson causes a stir when it is auctioned for £400,000 after a fierce bidding war - and hours later, its former owner is tortured and killed. When a Hogson work is then stolen, Barnaby and Jones research the Midsomer painter with the help of art teacher Matilda Simms. They soon uncover evidence of forgery - but the killer is still at large. Guest starring David Bamber and Susannah Harker, with John Nettles and Jason Hughes(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: As Foyle and his team search for a missing boy, their investigation leads them to a less than inviting hotel - and the discovery of the youngster's body in the nearby woods suggests one of the residents could be responsible. programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Local boxer John Kinsella's successful world title bid stirs up hidden passions among the residents of Midsomer Morchard. Detective drama, guest starring Phil Daniels (Quadrophenia, EastEnders), with John Nettles and Jason Hughes(n)

programme.desc: The newly promoted Detective Sergeant Morse investigates when a famous international thief tries to steal a Faberge egg from an auction. As he struggles with his new responsibilities, he finds himself forced to mentor a younger partner (played by Poldark's Lewis Peek)(n)

He tracks down the culprit, who swears he has turned over a new leaf, and decides not to charge him - a decision that shocks fellow sleuth DS Maud, whose misgivings are later justified in tragic fashion. Starring David Jason, Peter Gunn, Susannah Doyle, Bruce Alexander, John Lyons and Gwyneth Powell(n)

programme.desc: Crafty entrepreneur Arthur attempts to sell off cheap reproduction pottery to a Brighton antiques dealer, but gets drawn into a shady horse-racing deal instead. George Cole and Dennis Waterman star(n)

programme.desc: A bad dream has Regan's girlfriend fearing for his safety, and her visions ring true when he gets involved in a case involving a stolen lorryload of cigars. programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: CI5 investigates the fatal shooting of a South American president's son at a polo match, and finds that the victim's step-sister is unknowingly dating one of the terrorists involved. programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: When a conman and his girlfriend trick a prominent scientist with a tale about hidden treasure, he is taken hostage in Hamburg and sold to the Russians. Templar is 'lured' along similar lines and the confidence trick enables the spy to find the missing man. Roger Moore stars(n)

programme.title: Kojak
programme.desc: One of Theo's colleagues discovers that an old school friend is involved with fencing the loot from a diamond heist, and decides to exploit his friendship to bring the crooks down(n)

programme.title: Quincy ME
programme.desc: A friend of Quincy's is suspected of arson and murder following an attack on a furniture warehouse, and the trial leads to friction between the coroner and the Federal Grand Jury(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: A professional gambler needs to hide a group of people who might be able to help him win at roulette, so Arthur hits on the idea of using Terry's flat. Comedy drama, guest starring Anthony Valentine and featuring a pre-Birds of a Feather Lesley Joseph(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Bodie's schoolteacher girlfriend seeks CI5's assistance to quash a drug-smuggling ring after learning a 14-year-old pupil is involved having used then hidden a discarded firearm. The body count soon increases at an alarming rate as potential witnesses to a crime are killed off by the ruthless gang. Lewis Collins, Martin Shaw and Celia Gregory star(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: A former criminal and friend of Regan's wins a large amount of money on the pools - but then receives blackmailing phone calls regarding a crime from 11 years earlier for which he was never caught. Hard-hitting drama, guest starring Edward Judd, with John Thaw and Dennis Waterman(n)

programme.title: Rugby World Cup 2019 Highlights
programme.desc: Japan v Russia. Jill Douglas presents action from the opening match of the tournament, as the Pool A teams met at Tokyo Stadium. With commentary from Miles Harrison, Shane Williams and Jamie Roberts(n)

programme.desc: Vicki Butler-Henderson and Alex Riley choose their favourite car from 1964, then try to convince the expert panel of judges that their choice is best(n)

programme.title: Goodwood Revival
programme.desc: The glory days of motor racing are re-lived as classic cars from the 1940s, 50s and 60s race at Goodwood(n)

programme.title: Lethal Weapon 2 (1989)
programme.desc: Headstrong cop Martin Riggs and his cautious partner Roger Murtaugh stumble across a plot by a South African diplomat to launder drugs money by shipping gold coins into the US via his embassy. Action thriller sequel, with Mel Gibson, Danny Glover, Joe Pesci, Joss Ackland, Derrick O'Connor and Patsy Kensit(n)

programme.title: Hornblower
programme.desc: Hornblower's resourcefulness turns the attack on the Spanish-held fortress into a famous victory, but Buckland's treachery puts his life into further danger. Surviving a risky mission to blow up the fortifications and an attempt by the prisoners to take command of the Renown, the hero faces the death penalty for pushing Captain Sawyer into the hold - and hopes Archie's dying words can save him from the noose. Naval adventure, based on the stories by CS Forester, and starring Ioan Gruffudd(n)

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Simon Templar encounters a deadly lady with a gun and an attitude - and soon finds himself caught in the middle of an all-out revolution. Starring Roger Moore(n)

programme.title: Kojak
programme.desc: Theo breaks up a bar fight that was instigated by the son of a crime lord. The humiliated aspiring mobster wants the detective dead, and defies his father to put out a contract(n)

programme.title: Quincy ME
programme.desc: A man kidnaps a girl on her way home from school and tries to pull off the perfect crime by exploiting a legal loophole and demanding a ransom(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Crafty entrepreneur Arthur attempts to sell off cheap reproduction pottery to a Brighton antiques dealer, but gets drawn into a shady horse-racing deal instead. George Cole and Dennis Waterman star(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: A bad dream has Regan's girlfriend fearing for his safety, and her visions ring true when he gets involved in a case involving a stolen lorryload of cigars. The plot thickens when illicit cocaine is retrieved and arms stolen from the US are discovered to be on their way to a military faction in Ireland. John Thaw and Dennis Waterman star(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: CI5 investigates the fatal shooting of a South American president's son at a polo match, and finds that the victim's step-sister is unknowingly dating one of the terrorists involved. Ruby Wax and Pierce Brosnan feature in early screen appearances, alongside stars Lewis Collins, Martin Shaw and Gordon Jackson(n)

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: When a conman and his girlfriend trick a prominent scientist with a tale about hidden treasure, he is taken hostage in Hamburg and sold to the Russians. Templar is 'lured' along similar lines and the confidence trick enables the spy to find the missing man. Roger Moore stars(n)

programme.title: Kojak
programme.desc: One of Theo's colleagues discovers that an old school friend is involved with fencing the loot from a diamond heist, and decides to exploit his friendship to bring the crooks down(n)

programme.title: Quincy ME
programme.desc: A friend of Quincy's is suspected of arson and murder following an attack on a furniture warehouse, and the trial leads to friction between the coroner and the Federal Grand Jury(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: A professional gambler needs to hide a group of people who might be able to help him win at roulette, so Arthur hits on the idea of using Terry's flat. Comedy drama, guest starring Anthony Valentine and featuring a pre-Birds of a Feather Lesley Joseph(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Bodie's schoolteacher girlfriend seeks CI5's assistance to quash a drug-smuggling ring after learning a 14-year-old pupil is involved having used then hidden a discarded firearm. The body count soon increases at an alarming rate as potential witnesses to a crime are killed off by the ruthless gang. Lewis Collins, Martin Shaw and Celia Gregory star(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: A former criminal and friend of Regan's wins a large amount of money on the pools - but then receives blackmailing phone calls regarding a crime from 11 years earlier for which he was never caught. Hard-hitting drama, guest starring Edward Judd, with John Thaw and Dennis Waterman(n)

programme.title: Rugby World Cup 2019 Highlights
programme.desc: Japan v Russia. Jill Douglas presents action from the opening match of the tournament, as the Pool A teams met at Tokyo Stadium. With commentary from Miles Harrison, Shane Williams and Jamie Roberts(n)

Paul Verhoeven's sci-fi thriller, starring Arnold Schwarzenegger, Rachel Ticotin and Michael Ironside(n)

programme.desc: Officers suspect there are drugs hidden in a man's bag, and a travelling musician, who claims to possess special powers enabling her to foretell the future, struggles to get through immigration. Meanwhile. Biosecurity officials discover an unusual concoction in the bag of a traveller(n)

programme.title: Nothing to Declare
programme.desc: A man is caught red-handed by customs and tries to blame his travelling companion, but officials suspect they could be working together. Meanwhile, an immigration raid targets illegal workers and a swoop on a taxi rank catches the drivers with no work rights. Plus, a father and son from China have four suitcases full of undeclared food and a stimulant drug is found in picture frames that have arrived by mail(n)

programme.title: Stop, Search, Seize
programme.desc: Documentary following the work of Ireland's border force officers as they fight to stop gangs of organised smugglers from bringing drugs, money, guns, cars and even exotic animals into the country(n)

programme.title: Without a Trace
programme.desc: When a Hispanic boy is abducted from a Spanish Harlem street, Jack and the team are perplexed as to why family members have not come forward to help look for him. Drama, starring Anthony LaPaglia and Poppy Montgomery(n)

programme.title: Stargate SG-1
programme.desc: Jack and the team offer sanctuary to former Goa'uld conqueror Apophis, but his presence attracts the attentions of an old enemy, whose wrath and destructive power soon make them regret their generosity. Sci-fi adventure, starring Richard Dean Anderson(n)

programme.title: Hawaii Five-0
programme.desc: The team tries to ascertain why highly skilled thieves should steal an armoured car but leave all the money behind. The detectives locate a woman they believe is part of the crew, and Danny is forced to call in a favour from his ex-wife to keep the suspect under surveillance(n)

programme.title: MacGyver
programme.desc: The team joins the hunt when the young protegee of Mac's old science teacher is kidnapped, and have to rescue her armed with just pliers and speaker wire(n)

programme.title: Real Crime
programme.desc: The five-and-a-half-year investigation into the rape and murder of Southampton teenager Hannah Foster in 2003. After fleeing the country, Maninder Pal Singh Kohli confessed to the crime live on Indian TV, and despite a retraction and efforts to stop himself facing justice in the British courts, in 2008 he became the first Indian national to be extradited back to the UK. Featuring interviews, archive footage and dramatic reconstruction(n)

programme.title: Hawaii Five-0
programme.desc: McGarrett and the team once again become entangled in the twisted mind games of serial killer Dr Madison Gray when she stumbles into HPD covered in blood that matches Alicia Brown's and claims amnesia(n)

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: The agents try to track down three missing Afghanistan soldiers before their captors force them to pass on classified information. Meanwhile, Sam's colleagues worry that he has returned to work too soon. US adventure set in the high-stakes Office of Special Projects, starring Chris O'Donnell and LL Cool J(n)

programme.title: Banged Up Abroad
programme.desc: Chicago Outfit enforcer Frank Cullotta discusses crime boss Tony Spilotro, including his life in the mob and the burglary that led to his capture(n)

programme.title: Brit Cops

programme.desc: Designer Ann Hazell and the team are in Rochdale, Greater Manchester, to surprise a grandmother-of-nine whose husband of 48 years died recently. Sheila's family have decided that a home makeover would provide a fresh start and give her a much-needed boost, and the crew has just one hour to complete the task(n)

programme.title: 60 Minute Makeover
programme.desc: Scott Waldron and the team travel to Egham, Surrey, to transform the home of a mother-of-two with multiple sclerosis. Thirty years have passed since the house was last decorated and her condition means the need for a redesign has become more pressing. The crew focuses on updating her living room, dining room and bedroom with fresh and practical touches(n)

programme.title: 60 Minute Makeover
programme.desc: The programme heads to Chessington, Surrey, to provide a home makeover for a mother-of-two acting as a full-time carer for her 11-year-old daughter, who has a muscle-wasting disease. Money is tight for Sasha, so decorating has been low on her list of priorities. Enter Linda Barker and the team, who have one hour to transform her living room and two bedrooms(n)

programme.title: 60 Minute Makeover

The crew has one hour to provide a new look for Julie's living and dining rooms along with two bedrooms. Presented by Catherine Gee(n)

programme.title: 60 Minute Makeover

programme.title: 60 Minute Makeover

programme.title: Motorway Patrol
programme.desc: Documentary following members of the New Zealand highway patrol as they witness some of the best and worst behaviour on the nation's motorways. The series is centred around the roads of Auckland, the country's largest urban area, and features motoring chaos including major traffic collisions, escaped animals and pedestrians on busy roads. There is also footage of drivers falling asleep at the wheel, overloaded vehicles and suspects caught with illegal substances - plus a host of excuses as the people pulled over try to persuade officers they have not done anything wrong(n)

programme.title: Monkey Life

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: Alison and Jeremy rescue a squirrel monkey called Samantha from a derelict farm, a young chimp tries to settle in a new set, and the bachelor chimps show off their sporting prowess(n)

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: Samantha the squirrel monkey gets the chance to meet others of her kind after getting the all-clear from the park's vet. programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: The chimps make snowballs as winter grips the park, and Jeremy keeps an all-night vigil as the condition of Charlie the chimp takes a turn for the worse(n)

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: Alison Cronin visits Dao Tien, Monkey World's sister centre in Vietnam, which was founded in 2008, and has since become the site of a pioneering rescue and rehabilitation mission for golden-cheeked gibbons. Here, the team faces trouble during an attempt to save a rare silver langur monkey, and director Marina asks human doctors to perform ground-breaking eye surgery on a gibbon(n)

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: A mysterious accident puts a baby monkey's life in danger, while snowfall keeps most of the chimps inside, where they tuck into jacket potatoes to help keep the winter chills away(n)

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: Alison goes to great lengths when Trudy falls victim to a mysterious condition. Marcel the marmoset demonstrates small monkeys can be as clever as the larger apes, and baby woolly monkey Diego is allowed to join the adults(n)

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: Spanish capuchin monkeys Babe and Amy try to make new friends, while two others receive a lesson on wild behaviour. Elsewhere, Alison Cronin heads for Belfast to pick up a ring-tailed lemur that took over a family home(n)

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: Golden-cheeked gibbon Peanut presents her newborn, Jeremy confirms orang-utan Hsiao-Quai is pregnant - and the team suspects woolly monkey Yarima is, too(n)

programme.title: David Attenborough's Galapagos
programme.desc: The broadcaster explores the Galapagos, a collection of volcanic islands in the Pacific known for its extraordinary wildlife. More than 150 years after Charles Darwin formulated his ground-breaking theory of evolution on the remote archipelago, Attenborough explains how life on the islands has continued to evolve in biological isolation, examining how the ever-changing volcanic landscape has given birth to species and sub-species that exist nowhere else in the world. He begins by revealing how Galapagos rose from the ocean four million years ago(n)

programme.title: Filthy Cities
programme.desc: Dan Snow experiences what New York was like at the height of the Industrial Revolution. Millions fled to escape the persecution, poverty and famine of 19th-century Europe, only to be confronted by living conditions worse than those they had left behind. He explores the overcrowded slums of Lower Manhattan, which were a breeding ground for cholera and typhoid, and has a go at cooking using clothes dye and floor cleaner - ingredients that were added to meals to disguise the smell of spoiled meat. He also discovers how certain feats of engineering transformed not only the city, but the world(n)

programme.title: Fit to Rule: How Royal Illness Changed History
programme.desc: Historic Royal Palaces chief curator Lucy Worsley explores how the physical and mental health of Britain's monarchs has shaped the history of the nation, examining medical problems, doctors' reports, personal correspondence and possessions to gain an insight into the real men and women behind the royal portraits. She begins by examining the Tudor and Stuart dynasties, including the difficulty Henry VIII experienced producing a male heir, the cause of his daughter Mary I's phantom pregnancy and the special orthopaedic boots worn by Charles I(n)

programme.title: Winston Churchill: Winning the War, Losing the Peace
programme.desc: A look at Winston Churchill's battle to be elected Prime Minister just weeks after VE Day. The war leader was confident of victory, but ended up being humiliated at the polls with the Conservative party almost annihilated. Surprising revelations from first-hand witnesses, including Sir Max Hastings, Juliet Gardiner, Anthony Beevor and Dave Douglas, help to uncover whether Churchill's rejection was a mark of ingratitude, or the most mature decision ever made by a democracy(n)

The host Oksana Boyko strives to depart from the traditional Q-and-A form of interview in favor of an emotive and engaging conversation.
